name: Home
title: Home
description: Landing page that shows an overview of HYIP programs (featured, new, and problematic) and provides a global search entry point and navigation to listings, program details, forum, dashboard, saved programs, and settings.

core_features: Load a home overview dataset via getHomeOverview and display featured programs, new programs, recent scam alerts, and summary counts.
Provide a global search form that accepts a query and optional type filter, then navigates to search_results.html with the appropriate URL parameters.
Optionally call searchSite for lightweight, inline search suggestions (programs and forum threads) while typing in the global search bar.
Allow navigation from featured and new program cards to program_detail.html using the program id from the loaded data.
Provide quick navigation to HYIP Listings (all programs) and Problematic Programs views in programs.html through dedicated entry points on the page.
Provide navigation to Forum, Dashboard, Saved Programs, and Settings via the shared header.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Review overall HYIP state from the home page
1. User opens index.html without additional URL parameters.
2. On page load, the home-overview-loader component invokes getHomeOverview with no parameters.
3. The response provides featured_programs, new_programs, recent_scam_alerts, and summary_counts.
4. summary-counts-strip displays aggregate numbers from summary_counts (e.g., total_paying_programs, total_problematic_programs, total_new_programs_7d).
5. featured-programs-list and new-programs-list display program cards based on the featured_programs and new_programs arrays.
6. recent-scam-alerts-list displays recent_scam_alerts items with both program and latest_risk_alert data.
7. User can click through to any program_detail.html page from these sections for deeper inspection.

Workflow: Navigate to full HYIP listings from home
1. User opens index.html.
2. User clicks the main 'HYIP Listings' link in the header-navigation component or any primary CTA on the home page associated with all listings.
3. The click triggers navigation to programs.html with URL parameters view=all_programs.
4. The programs.html page (not part of this spec) uses its own logic to load and render filtered listings based on view=all_programs.

Workflow: Focus on problematic programs from home
1. User opens index.html.
2. User either clicks the 'Problematic Programs' link in header-navigation or a dedicated link in recent-scam-alerts-list.
3. The click triggers navigation to programs.html with URL parameters view=problematic_programs.
4. The programs.html page (not part of this spec) interprets view=problematic_programs and shows only problematic programs.

Workflow: Use global search to find a specific program or discussion
1. User opens index.html.
2. User types into the global-search-bar input.
3. Once the query satisfies the minimum length rule defined by the implementation, the search-suggestions-panel may call searchSite with parameters: query set to the current input value, type_filter determined by the search bar state (or left unspecified for mixed results), page set to 1, and page_size defined by the component configuration.
4. searchSite returns program_results and forum_thread_results with totals; search-suggestions-panel displays a concise suggestion list under the search field.
5. User can either select a suggestion (e.g., click a program result to navigate directly to program_detail.html?id=program.id or a forum thread result to navigate to forum.html with the appropriate thread context) or ignore suggestions and submit the full search.
6. On submit of the global-search-bar form, the page performs a navigation to search_results.html with URL parameters: query set to the current input value, type_filter set to the currently chosen filter or auto-detected type, and page=1.
7. search_results.html (not part of this spec) uses searchSite to render the full search results list.

Workflow: Jump from home into forum or personal areas
1. User opens index.html.
2. User uses header-navigation to click on 'Forum', 'Dashboard', 'Saved Programs', or 'Settings'.
3. The page navigates respectively to forum.html, dashboard.html, saved_programs.html, or settings.html without additional parameters (unless added by those pages).

Workflow: Inspect a single highlighted program from home
1. User opens index.html and waits for getHomeOverview to complete.
2. In featured-programs-list or new-programs-list, user clicks on a specific program card.
3. The click event reads program.id from the card data and triggers navigation to program_detail.html?id=program.id.
4. program_detail.html (not part of this spec) uses the id parameter to load and display the full program information.
interactions:
Interaction: Load home overview data
- Trigger: Page load (index.html initial render).
- Behavior: home-overview-loader component calls getHomeOverview.
- Result: The returned data is distributed to summary-counts-strip, featured-programs-list, new-programs-list, and recent-scam-alerts-list via props or state, causing them to render dynamically.

Interaction: Global search input typing
- Trigger: User types into the search input of global-search-bar.
- Behavior: Component updates its internal query state; optionally, when the query meets the minimum criteria, it calls searchSite (via search-suggestions-panel) to fetch suggestions.
- Result: search-suggestions-panel displays up-to-date program and forum thread suggestions beneath the search bar. If the query becomes empty or is cleared, suggestions are hidden and no further calls are made until the criteria are met again.

Interaction: Global search submit
- Trigger: User submits the search form via a button click or pressing Enter within the search input.
- Behavior: Prevents inline processing of full results on this page and instead navigates to search_results.html, constructing a URL that includes query and type_filter parameters, and sets page=1.
- Result: Browser loads search_results.html?query={currentQuery}&type_filter={currentTypeFilter}&page=1.

Interaction: Click on a featured or new program card
- Trigger: User clicks any program card within featured-programs-list or new-programs-list.
- Behavior: The click handler reads the bound Program.id from the card data.
- Result: Browser navigates to program_detail.html?id={Program.id}.

Interaction: Click on a recent scam alert item
- Trigger: User clicks an item or linked program name in recent-scam-alerts-list.
- Behavior: The click handler reads the associated Program.id from the item.
- Result: Browser navigates to program_detail.html?id={Program.id}.

Interaction: Click to view all HYIP listings
- Trigger: User clicks a 'HYIP Listings' link in header-navigation or a main link/button on the home page associated with viewing all programs.
- Behavior: Navigation is performed to programs.html with a view parameter.
- Result: Browser navigates to programs.html?view=all_programs.

Interaction: Click to view problematic programs
- Trigger: User clicks a 'Problematic Programs' link in header-navigation or within recent-scam-alerts-list.
- Behavior: Navigation is performed to programs.html with view=problematic_programs.
- Result: Browser navigates to programs.html?view=problematic_programs.

Interaction: Header navigation to other sections
- Trigger: User clicks 'Forum', 'Dashboard', 'Saved Programs', or 'Settings' in header-navigation.
- Behavior: Browser navigates to the corresponding page (forum.html, dashboard.html, saved_programs.html, settings.html) without extra parameters.
- Result: The relevant page is loaded and manages its own state.

Interaction: Select suggestion from search-suggestions-panel
- Trigger: User clicks a program suggestion.
- Behavior: The click handler reads Program.id from the suggestion item.
- Result: Browser navigates to program_detail.html?id={Program.id}.
- Alternative: User clicks a forum thread suggestion; the click handler reads ForumThread.id and category/thread context and navigates to forum.html with parameters defined by the forum page (outside this spec).
page_functionality.state_logic:
The Home page (index.html) does not require or interpret any incoming URL parameters for its own state; it always calls getHomeOverview with no arguments on initial load. Internal state for this page consists of: (1) home overview data returned from getHomeOverview; and (2) the current query and optional type filter in the global-search-bar, which are held in component state and not reflected in the URL for this page. All cross-page state is propagated via URL parameters when navigating away from Home:
- Navigating to programs.html with a full listing view sets view=all_programs in the URL.
- Navigating to programs.html with a problematic listing view sets view=problematic_programs in the URL.
- Navigating to program_detail.html passes the program identifier as id={Program.id}.
- Submitting the global search form navigates to search_results.html with query={currentQuery}, type_filter={currentTypeFilter or auto-detected type}, and page=1.
- Navigating to forum.html, dashboard.html, saved_programs.html, or settings.html occurs without additional URL parameters mandated by the Home page; any further parameters are owned by those target pages. The Home page does not read from or write to localStorage or other client-side persistent stores; all shareable state is expressed via URL parameters on navigation.

components.functionality: Provides top-level navigation links to Home, HYIP Listings (all programs), Problematic Programs, Forum, Dashboard, Saved Programs, and Settings, and hosts the global-search-bar component.
Calls getHomeOverview on initial render and distributes the returned featured_programs, new_programs, recent_scam_alerts, and summary_counts to child components.
event_handlers: onHomeOverviewLoadSuccess (stores data in page-level state and propagates to dependent components)
onHomeOverviewLoadError (sets an error state for dependent components to handle gracefully)

components.functionality: Displays aggregated counts of programs from summary_counts to give the user a quick snapshot of the current HYIP ecosystem.
event_handlers: onClickTotalPayingPrograms (optional; may navigate to programs.html?view=all_programs with status filter applied by the target page, outside this spec)

components.functionality: Renders a list of featured HYIP programs returned from getHomeOverview, each as a clickable card showing key metrics and a status label, and allows navigation to the program detail page.
Displays newly added HYIP programs based on the new_programs array from getHomeOverview, allowing users to quickly discover the latest entries and navigate to their detail pages.
event_handlers: onClickViewAllNewPrograms (navigates to programs.html?view=all_programs with any date-based filtering handled in the target page, outside this spec)

components.functionality: Shows recent scam or high-risk alerts for programs, using the recent_scam_alerts array from getHomeOverview, and allows users to jump directly to those program detail pages or to the problematic programs listing.

Provides a site-wide search input and optional type filter selector. On submit, it navigates to the search_results.html page with the query and filter encoded in URL parameters. It also coordinates with search-suggestions-panel to show inline suggestions using the searchSite interface.
data_binding: searchSite.parameters.query (derived from current input value when suggestions are requested)
searchSite.parameters.type_filter (derived from selected type filter when suggestions are requested)
searchSite.parameters.page (set to 1 when suggestions are requested)
searchSite.parameters.page_size (set by component configuration when suggestions are requested)
event_handlers: onChangeQueryInput (updates internal query state and notifies search-suggestions-panel when conditions are met)
onFocusSearchInput (may trigger initial load of suggestions for the current query via search-suggestions-panel)

components.functionality: Displays lightweight search suggestions (programs and forum threads) below the global-search-bar while the user types, using the searchSite interface. Suggestions are intended for quick navigation without leaving the Home page.
event_handlers: onRequestSuggestions (invokes searchSite with current query, optional type_filter, page=1, and configured page_size)
onClickForumThreadSuggestion (navigates to forum.html with parameters determined by the forum section, outside this spec)
onBlurHideSuggestions (hides suggestion panel when focus is lost, depending on UX rules)

components.functionality: Reusable component used internally by featured-programs-list and new-programs-list to render a single program summary card with key metrics and status, and to handle navigation to the program detail page.

layout.grid_system: Implicit single-column fluid layout, likely based on simple block elements or a basic HTML table for some parts rather than a formal grid system.
layout.layout_pattern: Use a single, linear, top-to-bottom column of stacked sections: header navigation at the very top, then a compact global search row, then a thin summary counts strip, and below that a large main content area dominated by three stacked text lists (featured, new, scam alerts). The search suggestions and loader are inline or immediately adjacent to the elements they relate to, never breaking the vertical flow. The program-card is rendered as a single dense row inside any program list.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Use a single, linear, top-to-bottom column of stacked sections: header navigation at the very top, then a compact global search row, then a thin summary counts strip, and below that a large main content area dominated by three stacked text lists (featured, new, scam alerts). The search suggestions and loader are inline or immediately adjacent to the elements they relate to, never breaking the vertical flow. The program-card is rendered as a single dense row inside any program list.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Reserve a thin horizontal band at the top (roughly the top 10–15% of the viewport height) for header navigation, search, and summary counts. Allocate the remaining vertical space (about 85–90% of the viewport) to the main list area. Apply modest left/right page margins of about 16–24px, with the central content stretching nearly the full browser width. Each list sits inside a simple bordered box with 8–12px internal padding, echoing the mockup’s main container behavior.
layout_strategies.content_density: Keep everything tight and information-dense: minimal vertical gaps (about 8px) between sections, small internal paddings, compact line-height, and no decorative whitespace. List items are closely stacked with minimal vertical padding so that many programs are visible at once. Form controls follow early-2000s defaults: small text fields and buttons with only 2–4px internal padding, labels placed inline in the flow.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Guide the eye straight down from left-aligned navigation at the top, to the search bar, then to the summary counts strip, and then down through the lists. Inside each list row, the eye flows left-to-right: ordinal number, then the program name link, then the domain link, then small gray metadata. The search suggestions panel appears directly below the search bar, so the eye only moves slightly downward to pick a suggestion before returning to the main vertical list scanning.

layout.overall_description: The page reads like an old-school, text-heavy investment forum home: a white background, black and blue text, and a single central column of content running from top to bottom. At the very top-left, a simple text-based navigation bar spans nearly the full width, with plain inline links. Just below, aligned with the same left margin, a compact global search bar runs horizontally, taking up most of the width but keeping a tight vertical footprint. Directly under the search, a thin summary strip shows total counts as a single line of text. Beneath that, the bulk of the page is a large main area comprising three stacked bordered boxes: featured programs on top, new programs in the middle, and recent scam alerts at the bottom. Each box is almost full-width, with only modest padding inside, and contains a dense vertical list of items rendered in the same minimalist, forum-like style—numbers, blue links, and small gray notes. A small inline loader message can appear near the top of this list area when data is being fetched. When users type in the global search, a simple rectangular suggestions panel drops down immediately under the search field, still within the same single-column alignment, offering a preview of programs in the same dense, textual manner. Every individual program instance (program-card) is just a tight, single-line row inside these lists, helping the entire page feel like a continuous, vertically scrollable table of text rather than a modern card grid.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The header navigation sits at the very top of the page, left-aligned and spanning nearly the full browser width, with about 16–24px margin from the viewport edges. It appears as a single horizontal line of text links (e.g., Home, Listings, Forum, Dashboard, Saved, Settings), laid out inline with small spaces between them, similar to an old forum header strip. There is a small vertical gap of about 8px below this navigation before the next section begins. The height is minimal—essentially a single line of text plus 4–8px padding above and below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block at the top of the document flow; it scrolls away with the page and is not sticky or fixed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The home overview loader appears as a short line of plain text such as “Loading data…” located just above the featured programs list’s first row, inside the main content column. When active, it occupies a single line at the top of the list area, aligned to the same left edge as the list content, with only about 4–8px vertical spacing above and below it. It does not have its own container; instead, it sits inline within the content flow, effectively acting as the first row inside the main lists section until data has loaded.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears and disappears inline within the normal flow, pushing list content down when visible; no fixed or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The summary counts strip sits directly below the global search bar, separated by a small 8px gap, and aligned to the same left and right bounds as the search. It is a single, thin horizontal bar of text (e.g., “Featured: 12 | New: 34 | Scam alerts: 5”), visually resembling a simple status line. There is no heavy styling—perhaps a slightly lighter gray text or subtle emphasis—but no background color. It spans roughly 80–90% of the page width, matching the lists and search width, with about 16–24px margin from the viewport edges. A small 8–12px vertical gap below it leads into the first bordered list container.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block in the vertical flow, neither sticky nor floating.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The featured programs list is the first major content box in the main area, positioned directly below the summary counts strip with an 8–12px vertical gap. It appears as a large, simple bordered rectangle stretching almost the full width of the content column (about 80–90% of the viewport width, centered with 16–24px side margins). Inside, there is 8–12px padding on all sides. At the top-left inside the box, a plain text heading such as “Featured Programs” is placed on its own line, possibly bolded. Below the heading, the list of programs flows vertically as a dense ordered or unstyled list. Each program entry (program-card) is a single line: ordinal number at the far left, a blue text link for the program name, a hyphen and another blue link for the domain, then a small gray parenthetical note for replies or status. The vertical spacing between items is minimal—just enough to separate lines, reflecting a forum-like list. The box’s height expands naturally based on the number of items, consuming a large portion of the visible viewport.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, expanding block within the page; scrollable only via the page, not within itself (no internal scroll).

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The new programs list sits directly below the featured programs list, separated by a small 8–12px vertical gap. It uses the same visual pattern: a simple rectangular box with a thin border, matching width and horizontal position with the featured list (about 80–90% of page width, centered within the main column). There is 8–12px internal padding. A heading like “New Programs” appears at the top-left inside the box, followed by a vertical list of program-card rows. Each row mirrors the featured list formatting: number, blue program name link, hyphen, blue domain link, and a small gray parenthetical note. The density is high, with tight line-height and minimal vertical padding, so the box reads as a continuation of the textual list style established above.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block in the vertical stack, expanding with content, scrolling with the rest of the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The recent scam alerts list is placed below the new programs list, again with a modest 8–12px vertical gap. It is another simple, nearly full-width bordered box aligned with the main column and the other lists. Internal padding is 8–12px all around. At the top-left inside the box, a heading such as “Recent Scam Alerts” appears, possibly in bold for emphasis. Below, scam alerts are listed vertically in the same compact, single-line style as program lists, though the text may indicate status or warnings. If program cards are reused here, each line still begins with a number and program link, followed by short alert-specific text in regular or slightly darker gray. The list remains dense, prioritizing showing many alerts in a small vertical space. This box typically sits near the bottom of the initial viewport but continues downward as alerts grow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, content-driven height; part of the continuous vertical scroll.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The global search bar is located beneath the header navigation, separated by an 8–12px vertical gap, and aligned with the main column. It appears as a single horizontal strip: a left-aligned label like “Search:” followed by a single-line text input field, possibly with a small submit button to its right. All elements are inline, mimicking an old forum login/search row. The input field stretches to cover most of the width (around 60–70% of the content width) but still feels compact due to small internal padding (2–4px) and standard browser styling. The entire search row spans about 80–90% of the page width and is centered within the page margins (16–24px from the viewport sides). There is a small 8px gap below the search bar before the summary counts strip.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static inline-block row in the vertical flow, with no sticky behavior; it scrolls away as the user moves down the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The search suggestions panel appears only when the user interacts with the global search input. It is visually anchored directly beneath the search field, sharing the exact same left alignment and width as the input box. The panel is a simple, white rectangular box with a thin border, slightly indented within the main column so it feels connected to the search. Inside, suggestions are shown as a dense vertical list of single-line rows—each resembling a mini program-card: program name as a blue link, optional domain text, and possibly a small gray note. Spacing between suggestions is tight, with 4–8px vertical separation. The panel’s height is limited to a few suggestion rows (e.g., 5–8), causing it to expand downward toward the summary counts strip and potentially overlap the area above the first list when long, but always staying within the single-column alignment.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily overlays or pushes down the content directly below the search input; positioned immediately under the input, behaving like an absolutely-positioned dropdown anchored to the input’s bottom edge while conceptually remaining within the same column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The program-card is rendered as a single, compact horizontal row of text inside any list (featured, new, or scam alerts). It occupies the full inner width of its parent list container, starting flush against the left padding. Each card reads left-to-right: a small ordinal number (e.g., “1.”) at the far left, followed by a blue text link for the program name, then a space, a hyphen, another space, and a blue link for the program’s domain. At the end of the line, aligned naturally with the flow, is a small gray parenthetical note such as “(23 replies)” or a status note. There is minimal vertical padding—just enough for legible line-height, with 2–4px above and below. Cards stack tightly on top of each other, forming a continuous list with very little vertical whitespace between rows, giving a utilitarian forum-thread feel rather than a modern card layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a normal block or list item within its parent container, expanding horizontally to the container width and stacking vertically with its siblings.

name: HYIP Listings
title: HYIP Listings
description: Lists HYIP programs with advanced filters, sorting, and a dedicated problematic-programs view. Users can refine results via URL-based filters, browse paying or problematic programs, save programs to personal lists, subscribe to scam alerts, and navigate to program detail pages or forum content.

core_features: Display a paginated list of HYIP programs based on the current view (all_programs or problematic_programs) and filters by calling getProgramsList.
Load available filter and sort options (status, payment methods, ROI range, minimum deposit range, running days range, payout rating range, sort options) using getProgramListingFilterOptions.

Allow users to filter programs by status, payment methods, daily ROI range, minimum deposit range, running days range, start date range, and minimum payout rating, with all filter changes reflected in URL parameters.
Allow users to sort listings via sort_by (e.g., rating, ROI, start date, running days) using sort options returned from getProgramListingFilterOptions and applied via getProgramsList.
Provide view toggles between all_programs and problematic_programs that update the view URL parameter and reload listings with appropriate default or user-selected filters.
Enable users to save or unsave programs to watchlist, bookmarks, or blacklist using saveProgram and removeSavedProgram, with saved state reflected in listing items.
Provide a problematic-programs-specific scam alert subscription/unsubscription control using toggleScamAlertsSubscription when view=problematic_programs.
Expose a global search input that uses searchSite to find programs and forum threads, with navigation to a search results page (outside this spec) based on the query.
Maintain listing state (filters, view, sorting, page) through URL parameters so that listing states are shareable and can be reloaded consistently without localStorage.
user_workflows:
Browse All Programs:
1) User opens programs.html without specifying view or with view=all_programs.
2) On page load, URL parameters are parsed and getProgramListingFilterOptions is called to populate filter controls.
3) getProgramsList is called with view=all_programs, filters derived from URL parameters, sort_by, and page.
4) User reviews the listing, adjusts filters or sorting as needed; each change updates URL parameters and triggers a new getProgramsList call.
5) User clicks a program title to open program_detail.html?id=program.id.

Browse Problematic Programs and Manage Scam Alerts:
1) User clicks the 'Problematic Programs' tab or a navigation link which routes to programs.html?view=problematic_programs.
2) Page parses URL parameters, calls getProgramListingFilterOptions, then calls getProgramsList with view=problematic_programs and current filters.
3) Listing shows only problematic programs according to application rules for this view (via server-side logic behind getProgramsList).
4) A scam-alert subscription control is visible; user clicks it to call toggleScamAlertsSubscription with subscribe=true or subscribe=false depending on current state.
5) On success, the control updates its label/state based on notifications_scam_alerts_in_site and the user remains on programs.html?view=problematic_programs (other URL parameters are preserved).

Filter and Sort Programs:
1) User adjusts one or more filter controls (e.g., selects status options, modifies ROI range, chooses payment methods, sets running days, start date range, or minimum payout rating).
2) On filter submission (or change, depending on interaction design), the page updates corresponding URL query parameters (status, payment_methods, min_daily_roi_percent, max_daily_roi_percent, min_min_deposit_usd, max_min_deposit_usd, min_running_days, max_running_days, start_date_from, start_date_to, min_payout_rating_value) and resets page=1.
3) The page calls getProgramsList with the updated filters, current view, and current sort_by.
4) User optionally changes sort_by; URL is updated with sort_by and page reset to 1; getProgramsList is called again.

Save Programs to Watchlist / Bookmarks / Blacklist:
1) User reviews the listing and for any program uses a save control (e.g., dropdown or buttons) on the program card.
2) When a save type is selected, the component calls saveProgram with programId and save_type (watchlist, bookmark, or blacklist), and optional notes.
3) On success, the program card updates its saved state (is_saved_watchlist, is_saved_bookmark, is_saved_blacklist) using returned data or via the next getProgramsList call.
4) To remove any saved state, the user chooses an unsave action which calls removeSavedProgram with the programId.
5) On success, the saved indicators on that card clear; a link is available to navigate to saved_programs.html for a full overview of saved items.

Use Pagination to Navigate the Listing:
1) User scrolls to pagination controls and clicks a page number or next/previous control.
2) The page parameter in the URL is updated to the selected page number; all other URL parameters (filters, view, sort_by) remain unchanged.
3) The page calls getProgramsList with the updated page value.
4) The listing updates to show items for the requested page.

Switch Between All and Problematic Views:
1) User clicks the 'All Programs' or 'Problematic Programs' tab.
2) The tab click sets view=all_programs or view=problematic_programs in the URL, preserves other filters where appropriate, and resets page=1 unless otherwise specified by implementation rules.
3) getProgramsList is called with the selected view and current filters, and the listing updates accordingly.
4) When switching to problematic_programs, the scam alert control becomes visible; when switching to all_programs, that control is hidden.

Run a Global Search from the Listings Page:
1) User types a query into the global search input in the header.
2) On submit, the page calls searchSite with query, optional type_filter, and page/page_size appropriate for the search results context.
3) Based on the project-wide pattern, either the user is navigated to a dedicated search results page with the query as URL parameter, or search results are displayed inline if that is the global behavior (outside the scope of this page but using searchSite as the interface).
interactions: On initial page load or when URL parameters change, the page controller parses all known query parameters, calls getProgramListingFilterOptions, then calls getProgramsList with view, a filters object built from URL parameters, sort_by, and page.
When the user clicks on a program title or main area of a program card, the browser navigates to program_detail.html?id=program.id using the program.id from the item data.
When the user clicks the 'All Programs' tab, the URL query parameter view is set to all_programs (other filter parameters may be preserved), and getProgramsList is called for the updated view.
When the user clicks the 'Problematic Programs' tab, the URL query parameter view is set to problematic_programs, and getProgramsList is called for the updated view.

When the user changes a filter control in the filter sidebar (status checkboxes, payment method options, ROI range input, minimum deposit range input, running days range input, date range selection, minimum payout rating input), the component updates the related URL parameters and triggers a refresh of the listing via getProgramsList.

When the user clicks a 'Clear' control for an individual filter or for all filters, the relevant parameters are removed from the URL query string (or reset to default), page is set to 1, and getProgramsList is called.
When the user selects a sort option from the sort dropdown, the sort_by query parameter is updated; page is set to 1; getProgramsList is called with the new sort_by.
When the user changes the page via pagination controls, the page query parameter is updated and getProgramsList is called with the new page value and existing filters and view.

When the user uses the save control on a program card to set a save type, saveProgram is called with programId and save_type; on success, the UI updates saved indicators and may optionally re-fetch the current page of results via getProgramsList.
When the user uses an unsave action on a program card, removeSavedProgram is called with programId; on success, the UI updates saved indicators and may optionally re-fetch via getProgramsList.

When the user clicks the scam alert subscription toggle in problematic_programs view, toggleScamAlertsSubscription is called with subscribe=true or subscribe=false depending on the intended state; on success, the control label/state updates based on notifications_scam_alerts_in_site from the response.
When the user clicks the 'View Saved Programs' link in any saved status summary component, the browser navigates to saved_programs.html.
When the user clicks the breadcrumb 'Home' link, the browser navigates to index.html.

When the user submits the global search form, searchSite is called with query and other relevant parameters; after that, navigation to a search results context happens as defined globally (outside this page).
page_functionality.state_logic:
The primary state of the HYIP Listings page is URL-driven. The page reads URL query parameters on load and whenever the URL changes, and uses them to construct the arguments passed to getProgramsList. The core URL parameters are:
- view: Controls which logical set of programs to load, with allowed values all_programs and problematic_programs. If missing, the implementation can define a default (commonly all_programs). This value is passed directly to getProgramsList as the view argument.
- status: Represents one or more Program.status values as a comma-separated string. On load, this string is split into an array and used to populate the filters.status_filter part of the getProgramsList filters argument. When the user changes the status filter, this parameter is rewritten in the URL.
- payment_methods: Represents one or more payment method values as a comma-separated string. On load, it is split into an array and applied to filters.payment_method_filter. Changing the payment methods filter updates this parameter.
- min_daily_roi_percent and max_daily_roi_percent: Numeric bounds for daily ROI filter. If present, they are parsed and passed as filters.min_daily_roi_percent and filters.max_daily_roi_percent to getProgramsList. If absent, the corresponding bound is treated as undefined. Filter controls update these parameters when changed.
- min_min_deposit_usd and max_min_deposit_usd: Numeric bounds for Program.min_deposit_usd. On load, they are parsed and passed as filters.min_min_deposit_usd and filters.max_min_deposit_usd. Filter controls maintain these values in the URL.
- min_running_days and max_running_days: Numeric bounds for Program.running_days. On load, they are parsed and passed as filters.min_running_days and filters.max_running_days. Changes to running days filters update these parameters.
- start_date_from and start_date_to: ISO datetime string bounds for Program.start_date. On load, they are parsed and mapped to filters.start_date_from and filters.start_date_to (as datetimes or strings depending on backend expectations). Date range controls write these values back into the URL.
- min_payout_rating_value: Numeric minimum for Program.payout_rating_value. On load, it is parsed and passed to filters.min_payout_rating_value. Filter controls update this parameter.
- sort_by: A string key for the active sort. On load, this is passed directly as sort_by to getProgramsList. Sort controls update this parameter.
- page: Numeric index of the current page. On load, this is passed as page to getProgramsList. Pagination controls update this parameter while leaving view, filters, and sort_by untouched.

Whenever any filter, sort, or view control updates the URL parameters, the page recomputes the filters object from the URL and calls getProgramsList again, ensuring that ProgramListingFilterState is consistent with URL state. The filters_applied object returned from getProgramsList (ProgramListingFilterState) can be used to normalize and display the active filters (e.g., if the backend adjusts ranges or interprets missing values), but the source of truth for persistence/sharing is always the URL. No filter or view state is stored in localStorage or similar mechanisms.

components.functionality: Coordinates initial data loading and subsequent refreshes based on URL parameters. On load and on URL changes, it parses query parameters, calls getProgramListingFilterOptions to load available options, constructs a filters object, and calls getProgramsList with view, filters, sort_by, and page. It then distributes the resulting data to child components (filters, listing, pagination, and scam alert controls).
Shows a simple breadcrumb trail for navigation (e.g., Home > HYIP Listings). Clicking the Home crumb navigates to index.html. The current page crumb is non-clickable.

Provides tabs or toggle controls for switching between the all_programs and problematic_programs views. It reads the current view from URL parameters and highlights the active tab. When a tab is clicked, it updates the view query parameter accordingly, may reset page to 1, and triggers the controller to call getProgramsList for the selected view.

Displays dynamic filter controls (status, payment methods, daily ROI range, minimum deposit range, running days range, start date range, minimum payout rating) using ranges and option lists from getProgramListingFilterOptions and the currently applied filters from filters_applied. It allows users to change filters and apply or clear them. It updates URL parameters without storing state in localStorage and triggers listing refreshes via the controller.

Summarizes active filters (e.g., selected statuses, active ranges, date bounds) in a compact list, using filters_applied from getProgramsList as the source of truth. Each summary item can be removed individually, which updates the corresponding URL parameter and triggers a refresh. A global 'Clear all' control may also be provided here as an alternate entry point.

Provides a sort dropdown populated with sort_options from getProgramListingFilterOptions and pagination controls using the pagination object returned from getProgramsList. Changing sort updates the sort_by URL parameter and resets page to 1. Pagination controls update the page parameter while preserving view and filters. Each change triggers getProgramsList via the controller.

Renders the list of programs returned in items from getProgramsList. Each item shows program name, status_label, daily_roi_display, min_deposit_display, payout_rating_display, running days, and saved state flags (is_saved_watchlist, is_saved_bookmark, is_saved_blacklist). It delegates item-level actions to child components such as navigation to details and save/unsave actions.

Represents a single program in the listing. Uses the program object and related display fields from getProgramsList to show core information and status. Provides controls for: navigating to program_detail.html?id=program.id, saving the program with a selected save_type, unsaving the program, and possibly showing a visual indication if it is in watchlist/bookmark/blacklist. It does not fetch data itself but invokes saveProgram or removeSavedProgram for save actions.

Optionally aggregates information about how many programs on the current page or in total are saved (watchlist, bookmarks, blacklist) based on is_saved_watchlist, is_saved_bookmark, and is_saved_blacklist flags returned in items from getProgramsList. Provides a 'View Saved Programs' navigation link to saved_programs.html.

Visible only when view=problematic_programs. Informs users about scam or problematic program alerts and provides a toggle button to subscribe or unsubscribe from in-site scam alerts. When clicked, it calls toggleScamAlertsSubscription with subscribe set based on the intended target state. It updates its visual state and messaging using notifications_scam_alerts_in_site and message from the response. The component does not assume the initial subscription state beyond what is provided by the surrounding context or previous toggle results.

Provides a search input for the entire site, accessible from this page header. On submit, it calls searchSite with the input query and any applicable type_filter, page, and page_size. Depending on broader site design, it may either navigate to a dedicated search results page (using query as a URL parameter) or allow the global controller to handle navigation.

layout.grid_system: Implicit single-column fluid layout, likely based on simple block elements or a basic HTML table for some parts rather than a formal grid system.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a strict, single-column, top-to-bottom flow. Every major component is stacked as a full-width block, left-aligned, with no decorative framing other than simple borders where needed. From top to bottom inside the content area: an invisible page-init controller wrapper, the global search bar, a thin scam-alerts banner, the breadcrumb navigation, the view-toggle tabs, the filter summary bar, the filter sidebar as a compact full-width filter form, the sort-and-pagination bar, and then the dominant programs-list container. Inside the programs-list, a small saved-status summary line sits at the top, followed by the ordered list of program-card rows, and a repeated sort-and-pagination bar at the bottom.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a strict, single-column, top-to-bottom flow. Every major component is stacked as a full-width block, left-aligned, with no decorative framing other than simple borders where needed. From top to bottom inside the content area: an invisible page-init controller wrapper, the global search bar, a thin scam-alerts banner, the breadcrumb navigation, the view-toggle tabs, the filter summary bar, the filter sidebar as a compact full-width filter form, the sort-and-pagination bar, and then the dominant programs-list container. Inside the programs-list, a small saved-status summary line sits at the top, followed by the ordered list of program-card rows, and a repeated sort-and-pagination bar at the bottom.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The outer page has modest left/right margins of around 16–24px, keeping the content as a fluid single column that can expand with viewport width. Vertically, every section is separated by small 8–12px gaps using the 4px base unit, preserving a compact stack. The programs-list container occupies the bulk of vertical space (around 70–80% of the scrollable content), while the search, alerts, breadcrumb, tabs, filters, and sort bars are slim utility strips at the top and above/below the list. Inside containers, padding is light (about 8–12px), with inline controls using minimal internal padding (around 2–4px) similar to default browser controls.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content is deliberately dense and utilitarian. Text lines are close together with a relatively tight line-height and minimal vertical padding per row. Forms are composed of labels and inputs placed in single horizontal rows where space allows, otherwise wrapping onto the next line without added breathing room. The programs list uses compact list items: each item is a single line of text whenever possible, with no extra card styling or vertical spacing beyond a small 4–8px gap. The overall effect is to show as many programs as possible in a single viewport while remaining legible.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye starts at the top-left with the global search input, then moves slightly right across the search submit button and scam-alert toggle, then drops down through breadcrumb, view-toggle tabs, filter summary, filters, and sort controls, before settling into a straight vertical scan down the ordered programs list. Within each program row, the eye tracks from the ordinal number at the far left to the blue program name link, then to the blue domain link, and finally to the smaller gray reply/metadata text at the right. All elements are left-aligned and textual, emphasizing a straightforward, downward reading flow with minimal lateral distraction.

layout.overall_description: The HYIP Listings page reads like an old-school, text-heavy forum index: a white background, black and blue text, thin gray rules, and basic browser-style form controls. The main column is centered within modest side margins and stretches fluidly across the viewport. At the top, a slim global search row and scam-alert toggle provide quick utilities. Immediately beneath, the breadcrumb line, view-toggle tabs, and filter summary form a tight stack of single-line text strips. A compact full-width filter box follows, then a utilitarian sort-and-pagination bar. The core of the page is a large, bordered box containing an ordered list of Programs, each as a terse single-line entry. A small saved-status summary text line sits at the top of this box, with sort and page controls repeated at the bottom. The entire page encourages scanning down a dense, vertical list of links, with subtle inline controls for refining and navigating results, all styled to feel like an early-2000s forum: no visual embellishment, only simple text and boxes.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page-init-controller is an invisible structural wrapper positioned at the very top of the main content column, just inside the page’s left/right margins. It occupies the full available width but does not introduce visible borders, background color, or text. Visually, it appears as if it is not there; functionally, it wraps all subsequent child components (search bar, alerts, breadcrumb, filters, list, etc.) in a single linear flow. It contributes no extra vertical spacing beyond the base top margin of roughly 16–24px between the browser edge and the first visible component.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Acts as a non-rendered layout container that spans 100% of the content width; it scrolls with the page and has no sticky or fixed behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The breadcrumb-nav is a single-line text strip positioned toward the top of the content stack, directly below the scam-alerts banner and above the view-toggle tabs. It spans the full width of the main content column and is left-aligned, with about 8–12px of vertical margin separating it from the components above and below. The text appears inline (e.g., "Home > HYIP Listings"), using normal-weight, black or dark gray text with small blue links for each breadcrumb segment. There is no box framing; at most, a very subtle bottom margin distinguishes it before the next section. The vertical height is minimal—roughly one line of text plus 4–8px padding top and bottom, matching the compact, forum-like style.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full-width text line; scrolls with the page and does not stick. It always appears above filters and list regardless of viewport height.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The view-toggle-tabs sit immediately beneath the breadcrumb-nav, separated from it by a small vertical gap (about 8px). They form a single horizontal row of plain text links, left-aligned, representing different listing views (for example: "All", "Paying", "Problematic"). Each tab looks like a standard inline link—blue text with an underline on hover—with no graphical tab chrome. Tabs are separated by simple delimiters such as vertical bars or hyphens ("All | Paying | Problematic"), in keeping with an old forum navigation style. The entire row spans the full width of the content column but uses only as much horizontal space as the text links need, leaving empty white space to the right. Vertical height is one text line with minimal 4px extra padding above and below. A selected tab may be indicated by bold text or the absence of a link underline, but still remains visually minimal.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full-width horizontal strip; it scrolls normally with the page and remains above filters and sorting. On narrow viewports, tabs wrap to a second line if necessary rather than shrinking.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Despite its name, the filter-sidebar is laid out as a full-width, stacked filter section rather than a side column, in order to respect the single-column forum layout. It is positioned beneath the filter-summary-bar with a small vertical gap (about 8–12px) and above the sort-and-pagination-bar. Visually, it appears as a lightly bordered box (1px gray border) on a white background, spanning nearly the full content width, with roughly 8–12px of internal padding on all sides. Inside, filter controls are arranged as compact rows: each row starts at the left with a label followed by a small text input, select dropdown, or checkbox group. Where there is room, multiple related controls share the same row horizontally; otherwise, they wrap to the next line without extra spacing. There are no large headings—at most a small, bold label like "Filters" in the top-left. The overall height remains as short as possible given the number of filters, with tight vertical gaps (4–8px) between rows to maintain high information density.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a standard full-width block within the vertical stack. It scrolls with the page, has no sticky behavior, and shrinks or grows in height depending on available filters and viewport width. On smaller screens, filter rows simply wrap, maintaining the stacked single-column feel.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The filter-summary-bar is a slim informational strip placed between the view-toggle-tabs and the filter-sidebar, with about 8px vertical spacing above and below it. It spans the full width of the main content column and is left-aligned. Visually, it is a plain text line like "Active filters: Paying, Min deposit > $50" with small blue "clear" or "reset" links inline. The text is rendered in normal body size or slightly smaller and might use a slightly lighter gray for non-interactive parts to differentiate it from primary navigation text. There is no visible border or background color difference; it appears as simple inline text in a single row. The height is essentially one line of text plus minimal 4px top/bottom padding.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A static full-width text bar that scrolls with the page; it does not stick or float. On narrow viewports, the summary text wraps to multiple lines, increasing the height modestly but without additional spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The sort-and-pagination-bar appears twice: once immediately above the programs-list container and again immediately below it, both times as a single horizontal strip. Each instance spans the full content width and sits with an 8px vertical gap from neighboring components (filters above, list container or page bottom below). Within the bar, content is arranged in a simple two-part layout: on the left, text and controls for sorting (for example, a label "Sort by:" followed by a basic HTML select dropdown, plus perhaps a secondary dropdown for order); on the right, compact pagination links such as "Prev 1 2 3 4 Next" in blue. The alignment is handled via simple inline text: the left cluster begins at the left margin, and the right cluster is floated or aligned to the far right, with a clear separation of whitespace between them. Vertical height is one line of controls, with about 4–8px padding above and below. There is no background color change; at most, a subtle gray horizontal rule can appear above or below the bar to separate it from the list.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard full-width block that scrolls with the page. The bar is not sticky; both instances move out of view as the user scrolls. On smaller screens, right-aligned pagination links may wrap under the sorting controls, forming two lines while still maintaining tight vertical spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The programs-list is the dominant element on the page, positioned immediately below the upper sort-and-pagination-bar and above the lower sort-and-pagination-bar. It appears as a large, rectangular container with a thin 1px gray border and white background, spanning nearly the full width of the content column (leaving only the outer page margins). Internal padding is around 8–12px on all sides. At the top inside this box, the saved-status-summary is aligned, and below it a numbered ordered list of program entries fills most of the container. Each program is a single <li> row in this list. The list continues vertically, consuming the majority of the page’s scrollable height. There are no inner dividing lines between items aside from the natural list structure; spacing between list items is minimal (about 4–8px vertical gap) to maximize density. The container height is flexible, expanding as many items as are available, with the rest of the page scrolling to accommodate. Overall, this box occupies roughly 70–80% of the vertical content area in a typical view.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A flexible-height, full-width container that scrolls normally. It anchors the core listing content and does not use any fixed or sticky behavior. On narrow viewports, the width contracts with the page, and text within list items wraps to a second line when necessary, but the structure remains a single-column ordered list.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each program-card is presented as a single, dense text row within the ordered list inside the programs-list container, not as a visually distinct "card". The left edge starts flush with the list’s text indent, showing the ordinal number generated by the ordered list (e.g., "1."). Immediately to the right of the number, separated by a single space, is the program name as a blue hyperlink. After the program name, there is a hyphen or dash, followed by the program’s domain as another blue hyperlink. At the far right of the same line, or immediately following the domain when space is tight, appears a smaller, gray, parenthetical note such as "(15 replies, 3 saved)". All of this content is in a single line where possible; on narrower screens, the gray parenthetical wraps to the next line directly below, maintaining proximity with minimal extra vertical spacing. There is no background color, no box around each item, and no extra padding beyond a small 4–8px vertical gap separating one list item from the next. The line-height is relatively tight to fit many items on screen. The overall effect is that of a classic forum topic list: numbers and blue links marching straight down the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a standard list item within the ordered list. It has no independent scrolling or fixed positioning. Each program-card expands in height only as needed if text wraps to multiple lines, but generally strives to remain a single compact row.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The saved-status-summary is a small informational snippet placed inside the programs-list container at its top, just below the container’s top padding and above the first program list item. It is aligned to the right side of the container for quick scanning, while still occupying only a single line of text in normal circumstances. The text might read something like "Saved programs: 5 (view list)" with counts in regular dark text and any actions (like "view list") as small blue links. The left side of the same horizontal line is left blank, or optionally may contain a subtle label like "HYIP Listings" to echo the page title, but overall the snippet remains unobtrusive. There is a small 4–8px vertical gap below this line before the first program item, preserving the dense layout while visually separating summary from list items.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static text anchored at the top of the programs-list container. It scrolls up and out of view together with the list as the user scrolls. On narrow viewports where the text cannot fit in one line, it wraps to two lines, gently increasing the height but maintaining tight spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The scam-alerts-banner is a slim notification and toggle strip located near the top of the page, directly beneath the global-search-bar and above the breadcrumb-nav. It spans the full width of the content column and is left-aligned. Visually, it reads like a short, plain text sentence such as "Scam alerts: [ ] Subscribe to notifications" where the checkbox and label use standard browser styles, and any related links are in blue. The text is regular body size, perhaps slightly smaller than main headings, and uses black or dark gray. There is no prominent background color; at most, it may use a faint gray underline or rule beneath it to subtly separate it from the sections below, but the overall appearance remains minimal and text-centric. Vertical height is essentially one line of text with about 4–8px spacing above and below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A static full-width bar that scrolls with the page and has no sticky behavior. On narrow viewports, the checkbox and text may wrap to a second line, but the component remains a tight, low-profile strip.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The global-search-bar sits at the very top of the visible content stack (beneath any unseen global header), just inside the page-init-controller’s boundaries. It spans the full width of the content column but uses only as much space as needed for its controls, leaving white space to the right. Layout is a simple horizontal row: a text label (optional) on the far left, followed by a wide text input box (occupying roughly 40–60% of the content width on typical screens), followed by a standard HTML submit button labeled "Search" or similar. All elements are aligned on a single baseline. Styling uses default browser form controls: no custom borders or rounded corners beyond the browser default. Vertical spacing is compact, with about 8–12px margin below before the scam-alerts-banner. This bar takes up a small vertical portion of the page (around 5–10% of the initial viewport height), ensuring it does not overshadow the programs list.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, non-sticky full-width form that scrolls with the rest of the page. On narrower viewports, the search input shrinks proportionally, and the button may move closer; if space is extremely constrained, the button can wrap to the line below, but spacing remains tight and utilitarian.

name: Program Detail
title: Program Detail
description: Shows full details of a single HYIP program, including investment plans, metrics, risk information, community discussion, reviews, polls, and personal save actions (watchlist, bookmarks, blacklist). Users can switch between overview, discussion, and poll tabs using the UI or the URL.

core_features: Load and display a single HYIP program’s details, plans, stats, risk info, and saved status using getProgramDetailView based on the id URL parameter.
Provide internal tab navigation (overview, discussion, poll) controlled by the tab URL parameter, with defaulting and tab-state updates via URL.
Display investment plans associated with the program (ProgramPlan list) and key metrics such as daily ROI, minimum deposit, running days, and 30-day total return using getProgramDetailView.

Allow the user to save the program as watchlist, bookmark, or blacklist, or remove any saved state, using saveProgram and removeSavedProgram, then optionally navigate to saved_programs.html with the appropriate tab.
Provide a Discussion/Reviews section that lists ProgramComment items with pagination and sorting using getProgramDiscussion, defaulted to newest comments.
Enable the user to write and submit a new comment or star-rated review (with optional rating) through a review form that calls postProgramReview.
Link commenter usernames in the discussion to user_profile.html and offer a shortcut action to compose a private message at message_compose.html, passing profile and program context via URL.
Host a community poll tab that loads a program-specific ProgramPoll and PollOption list using getProgramPoll, shows whether the user has voted, and allows voting via castProgramPollVote.
Include a global search input that calls searchSite for typeahead or preview (optional) and navigates to search_results.html with the query when submitted.
user_workflows:
View program overview:
1. User navigates to program_detail.html?id={Program.id} (optionally with &tab=overview).
2. Page-init logic reads id (and tab if present).
3. The page calls getProgramDetailView with programId = id.
4. On success, the overview tab is populated with Program details, stats_display, plans, risk_info, and saved_status.
5. If no tab parameter or an invalid value is provided, the page sets the active tab to 'overview' and updates the URL to program_detail.html?id={id}&tab=overview without reloading data.

Switch between Overview / Discussion / Poll tabs:
1. User clicks a tab label (Overview, Discussion, Poll).
2. Tab navigation updates the tab URL parameter (e.g., program_detail.html?id={id}&tab=discussion) via history/navigation.
3. If switching to Discussion and comments have not been loaded yet, the page calls getProgramDiscussion with programId = id and default sort_by (e.g., 'newest'), page, and page_size.
4. If switching to Poll and poll data has not been loaded yet, the page calls getProgramPoll with programId = id.
5. The corresponding tab panel is shown; others are hidden, while retaining any in-memory state (e.g., current discussion page).

Add or remove program from watchlist, bookmarks, or blacklist:
1. On the overview tab, the user clicks a save control (e.g., 'Add to Watchlist', 'Bookmark', or 'Blacklist').
2. The page calls saveProgram with programId = id, save_type set to the chosen type, and optional notes collected from the user.
3. On success, the saved_status section in the UI is updated to reflect the current save state (is_watchlisted, is_bookmarked, is_blacklisted) using the response and/or a refresh via getProgramDetailView.
4. The UI shows a contextual link like 'View Watchlist', 'View Bookmarks', or 'View Blacklist'.
5. If the user clicks that link, the browser navigates to saved_programs.html with tab set to watchlist, bookmarks, or blacklist as appropriate.
6. If the user chooses to remove all saved state, they click a 'Remove from saved' action, which calls removeSavedProgram with programId = id. On success, saved_status flags are cleared in the UI.

Read and navigate discussion / reviews:
1. User activates the Discussion tab (directly via URL tab=discussion or by clicking the tab).
2. If discussion data has not been loaded, the page calls getProgramDiscussion with programId = id, a default sort_by (e.g., 'newest'), and initial page/page_size.
3. The discussion panel displays a list of comments, each showing author_username, rating_stars (if present and is_review true), and body.
4. The user can change sort options (e.g., newest) if provided, which triggers a new getProgramDiscussion call with the updated sort_by while keeping the same programId.
5. The user can navigate through pagination controls, which update internal page state and call getProgramDiscussion with the new page value.
6. Clicking a commenter username navigates to user_profile.html?profileId={comment.author_profile_id}.

Post a review or comment:
1. On the Discussion tab, the user enters text in the review form body field.
2. Optionally, the user selects a star rating and a flag/checkbox to mark this as a review.
3. The user submits the form, triggering postProgramReview with programId = id, rating_stars (if selected), body, and mark_as_review reflecting whether it is a structured review.
4. On success, the new ProgramComment is added to the comment list in the UI (or the page refreshes the list via getProgramDiscussion with the current sort/page).
5. The form is cleared and any success message is shown.

Vote in the community poll:
1. User activates the Poll tab (URL tab=poll or tab click).
2. If poll data is not yet loaded, the page calls getProgramPoll with programId = id.
3. The poll panel displays poll.question and available options with vote_count if appropriate.
4. If ProgramPoll.has_user_voted is false, the user can select an option and click a 'Vote' action.
5. The page calls castProgramPollVote with pollId = poll.id and optionId = selected option id.
6. On success, the poll and options in the UI are updated with the returned poll and options; has_user_voted becomes true and the selected option is highlighted.
7. If the user has already voted, the UI prevents further voting and shows current results only.

Message or inspect a commenter:
1. On the Discussion tab, each comment displays the author_username as a link and a 'Message' shortcut action.
2. Clicking the author_username navigates to user_profile.html?profileId={comment.author_profile_id}.
3. Clicking the 'Message' action navigates to message_compose.html with recipientProfileId = comment.author_profile_id, relatedProgramId = program.id, and relatedCommentId = comment.id as URL parameters.
4. The called page can then pre-fill message context based on those parameters.

Use global site search from the Program Detail page:
1. The global search input is available in the top area of the page.
2. The user types a query term and submits the search form.
3. Optionally, the page can call searchSite with query set to the input value (and type_filter/page/page_size as needed) to display inline suggestions; this is not required for navigation.
4. For full search results, the page navigates to search_results.html with the query parameter set to the search term (and any additional filter parameters handled on that page).

Handle invalid or missing program id:
1. If the page is loaded without an id parameter or with an unusable value, the page does not call getProgramDetailView.
2. Instead, it shows an error state and a link back to index.html.
3. If getProgramDetailView is called but returns an error (e.g., program not found), the page also shows an error state while preserving the global search and breadcrumb navigation so the user can recover.
interactions: On page load, read id and tab URL parameters; if id is valid, call getProgramDetailView to fetch Program details, plans, stats_display, risk_info, saved_status, discussion_summary, and poll_summary.

User clicks on different tab labels (Overview, Discussion, Poll); this updates the tab URL parameter and, on first entry into a tab that requires additional data, triggers getProgramDiscussion or getProgramPoll as needed.
User clicks 'Add to Watchlist', 'Bookmark', or 'Blacklist'; this calls saveProgram with the corresponding save_type and updates the saved_status UI on success.
User clicks 'Remove from saved'; this calls removeSavedProgram with programId and clears all save indicators on success.
User clicks 'View Watchlist', 'View Bookmarks', or 'View Blacklist' after a save; this navigates to saved_programs.html with the tab URL parameter set according to the chosen list.

Within the Discussion tab, user changes sort controls or pagination controls; these actions call getProgramDiscussion with updated sort_by and/or page while keeping programId and page_size consistent.

User fills out the review form (text body and optional star rating) and submits; this calls postProgramReview and, on success, updates the comment list (either by adding the returned comment or by refetching via getProgramDiscussion).
User clicks a commenter username; this navigates to user_profile.html with profileId from comment.author_profile_id.
User clicks the 'Message' action next to a comment; this navigates to message_compose.html with recipientProfileId, relatedProgramId, and relatedCommentId set from the comment and current program.
Within the Poll tab, user selects a poll option and clicks a 'Vote' control; this calls castProgramPollVote and updates poll results and has_user_voted state on success.
User types into the global search bar and submits; this may optionally call searchSite for inline suggestions and then navigates to search_results.html with the query parameter.
User clicks breadcrumb 'Home'; this navigates to index.html.

If any interface call fails (getProgramDetailView, getProgramDiscussion, postProgramReview, getProgramPoll, castProgramPollVote, saveProgram, removeSavedProgram, searchSite), the page shows a non-blocking error message and offers retry controls where appropriate.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The primary page state is controlled via URL parameters and in-memory component state. The id parameter is required and identifies which Program to load. On initial load, if id is valid, the page calls getProgramDetailView(programId = id) to populate Program details, ProgramPlan list, stats_display, saved_status, discussion_summary, poll_summary, and risk_info. If id is missing or invalid, the page does not call the interfaces and instead shows an error with navigation back to index.html.

The tab parameter controls which main section is active: 'overview', 'discussion', or 'poll'. On initial load, the page reads tab. If tab is missing or not one of the known values, the page defaults to 'overview' and updates the URL to include tab=overview via navigation or history manipulation without reloading the program details. When the user clicks a tab, the page updates the tab parameter in the URL (e.g., program_detail.html?id={id}&tab=discussion) and activates the corresponding panel.

Data loading is lazy per tab: getProgramDetailView is always called once a valid id is present. The Discussion tab uses getProgramDiscussion; it is called the first time the Discussion tab becomes active and whenever the user changes sort_by or pagination. The current sort_by and pagination page are held as in-memory state within the Discussion tab; they are not persisted in URL parameters to keep the URL schema simple. The Poll tab uses getProgramPoll; it is called once when the Poll tab is first activated and again after a successful castProgramPollVote to refresh poll and options.

Saved state (watchlist, bookmark, blacklist) is not stored in the URL; instead, it is derived from the saved_status object returned by getProgramDetailView and updated by calls to saveProgram and removeSavedProgram. After such calls, the UI updates this state locally and may optionally re-call getProgramDetailView to ensure consistency.

Global search does not alter the Program Detail internal state; submitting the search navigates to a separate search_results.html page with its own URL parameters. Breadcrumb navigation similarly performs full-page navigation back to index.html.

No state is stored in localStorage or similar mechanisms; all persistent state relevant to this page is represented either by server-side data accessed through the interfaces (e.g., saved programs, poll votes, comments) or by the id and tab URL parameters.

components.functionality: Controls initial page load: reads id and tab from the URL, validates them, calls getProgramDetailView with programId, and sets the initial active tab. It also coordinates lazy loading of discussion and poll data when those tabs are first activated.
Displays a breadcrumb path including a link back to Home and the current program name. The Home link navigates to index.html without altering state on this page.

Shows key identifying information for the program such as Program.name, Program.status, risk_info.risk_label, and high-level stats summary. This component reacts to changes from getProgramDetailView and indicates if the program is problematic based on Program.is_problematic.

Displays computed stats from stats_display (daily ROI, minimum deposit, 30-day total return, running days, payout rating, payment methods) and any available risk_info. It does not compute values itself; it only renders the display-ready strings provided by getProgramDetailView.

Lists all ProgramPlan entries associated with the current Program, using details returned by getProgramDetailView. The component helps users compare plan types, durations, total returns, minimum and maximum deposits, currency, and principal_returned status.

Allows the user to manage personal save state for the program (watchlist, bookmark, blacklist). It reads saved_status from getProgramDetailView to show current state and exposes actions to add/update or remove saved entries by calling saveProgram and removeSavedProgram. After save/remove, it updates the visual state and offers navigation links to the appropriate tab in saved_programs.html.

Renders and controls tab selection for Overview, Discussion, and Poll sections. It reads the current tab from the URL, highlights the active tab, and when a tab is clicked, updates the tab parameter in the URL and notifies the init-controller to load any data required for the new tab.

Container panel for the Overview tab. It displays the program header, stats summary, plans, risk info, discussion_summary (e.g., total comment_count and latest_comment preview), and poll_summary (has_active_poll and question snippet). It uses only the data returned by getProgramDetailView and does not perform additional API calls.

Container panel for the Discussion/Reviews tab. On first activation, it triggers getProgramDiscussion to load comments. It holds in-memory state for the current sort_by and page. It displays the review list and the review form.

Provides controls for sorting comments (e.g., newest) and navigating between pages of comments. Changing sort or page triggers getProgramDiscussion with programId, the selected sort_by, and the target page.

Displays the list of ProgramComment records returned by getProgramDiscussion. Each item shows author_username, optional rating_stars when is_review is true, comment body, timestamps, and actions to view the author profile or send a message.

Collects user input to post a new comment or review. It has a text body field, optional star rating, and a control to mark the entry as a review. Submitting calls postProgramReview with programId, rating_stars (if any), body, and mark_as_review. On success, it clears the form and triggers an update in the discussion-tab-panel to refresh the comment list.

Container panel for the Poll tab. On first activation, it triggers getProgramPoll to load the active ProgramPoll and its PollOption list, then displays the poll question, options, and vote counts if available. It also respects ProgramPoll.has_user_voted to decide whether to allow voting.

Handles poll interactions. It shows poll.question and selectable options, highlights the option corresponding to ProgramPoll.user_selected_option_id, and displays aggregate vote counts. If has_user_voted is false, selecting an option and clicking the vote control calls castProgramPollVote with pollId and optionId. On success, it updates local poll and option data with the returned values and switches to results-only view.

Provides a site-wide search control on the Program Detail page. On submit, it can optionally call searchSite for inline suggestions and then navigates to search_results.html with the query parameter populated from the input field.

layout.grid_system: Implicit single-column fluid layout, likely based on simple block elements or a basic HTML table for some parts rather than a formal grid system.
layout.layout_pattern: Use a single, top-to-bottom, single-column flow. At the top of the content column, place the program header (page title) aligned left, with the global search bar aligned to the right on the same horizontal row. Directly beneath that row, place the breadcrumb as a full-width text line. Below the breadcrumb, introduce one large, bordered content box that contains all program-specific information: stats, plans, save controls, tab navigation, and tab content (overview, discussion, poll). Inside the box, maintain a strictly vertical stack: summary/statistics, plans, save controls, tab navigation, then whichever tab panel is active; tab panels share the same rectangular area and are swapped via the tab navigation.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Use a single, top-to-bottom, single-column flow. At the top of the content column, place the program header (page title) aligned left, with the global search bar aligned to the right on the same horizontal row. Directly beneath that row, place the breadcrumb as a full-width text line. Below the breadcrumb, introduce one large, bordered content box that contains all program-specific information: stats, plans, save controls, tab navigation, and tab content (overview, discussion, poll). Inside the box, maintain a strictly vertical stack: summary/statistics, plans, save controls, tab navigation, then whichever tab panel is active; tab panels share the same rectangular area and are swapped via the tab navigation.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Reserve only a small vertical slice at the top of the page for the header+search row and breadcrumb (roughly the top 10–15% of the viewport). The dominant vertical space is given to the bordered main content box, which spans almost the full width of the content column (about 80–90% of the viewport width) with modest left/right margins (~16–24px). Inside the box, space is compact: slim vertical gaps of 8–12px between sections, and 8–12px padding between the box border and its contents. The tab content area (overview/discussion/poll panels) occupies the majority of the box height, while smaller utility sections like save controls and tab navigation take up minimal vertical space.
layout_strategies.content_density: Maintain high information density. Use tight line-height for text, small vertical spacing (one 8px unit) between adjacent text blocks, and minimal padding around interactive elements.
