When the user changes the default currency selection and submits the currency form, updateAccountSettings is invoked with the new default_currency, and the component updates the selected value with the returned AccountSettings data.

When the user toggles watchlist risk/status notifications and submits the notifications form, updateAccountSettings is called with the updated notifications_watchlist_risk_in_site flag, and the toggle’s state is re-bound from the response.

When the user toggles the scam alerts subscription switch, the page immediately calls toggleScamAlertsSubscription with the new desired subscribe state; upon success, it updates the local view of notifications_scam_alerts_in_site and may also synchronize this value into AccountSettings used by the page.
When the user clicks the Review Scam List control inside the notifications section, the page navigates to programs.html?view=problematic_programs without calling additional interfaces.
When the user clicks a View Dashboard control from the settings page, the browser navigates to dashboard.html using a standard link or programmatic navigation without additional API calls.

When the user submits the header search form, searchSite is called with the provided query and optional type_filter and paging arguments; based on the response, the component navigates to a search results page with the query encoded in the URL.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary page-level state is derived from URL parameters and the latest AccountSettings from getAccountSettings. The section URL parameter controls which settings subsection is active: if section is present (for example, 'dashboard', 'currency', or 'notifications'), the page activates the matching tab and renders only that section’s form. If section is missing or does not match a known section, the page selects a default section and may update the URL accordingly. Changing tabs updates the section parameter in the URL via navigation or history APIs so that the state is restorable on reload and shareable as a link. The AccountSettings object returned by getAccountSettings is stored in page memory and is the single source of truth for form initial values. When updateAccountSettings succeeds, the returned settings object replaces the in-memory AccountSettings state so that all sections see consistent, up-to-date values. The scam alerts subscription state is maintained using both AccountSettings.notifications_scam_alerts_in_site and the response from toggleScamAlertsSubscription; after a successful toggleScamAlertsSubscription call, the returned notifications_scam_alerts_in_site is merged into the in-memory AccountSettings to keep all controls consistent. The page does not use localStorage or other persistent client-side storage; all persistent state is maintained by the backend via the provided interfaces and reloaded using getAccountSettings. Any navigation to external pages (dashboard.html, programs.html, search results) preserves user context using URL parameters such as section and view.

components.functionality: Orchestrates the Settings page lifecycle: parses the URL parameter section, calls getAccountSettings on load, distributes the AccountSettings data to child components, and coordinates updates after settings changes.
Displays and manages navigation between settings sections (such as dashboard, currency, and notifications) and keeps the active section synchronized with the URL parameter section.

Provides controls for dashboard-related preferences, including minimum daily ROI to display, whether closed programs should be shown, and which Program.status values should be included on the dashboard. Submits changes via updateAccountSettings.
Displays the current default display currency and allows the user to select a new one from the supported default_currency enum values. Persists changes via updateAccountSettings.

Manages general in-site notification preferences excluding the scam alerts subscription toggle. This includes enabling or disabling in-site notifications for watchlisted program status or risk level changes and saving the preference via updateAccountSettings.

Provides a dedicated switch for subscribing or unsubscribing from in-site scam alerts for problematic programs. Calls toggleScamAlertsSubscription directly when toggled and synchronizes the returned notifications_scam_alerts_in_site flag with the current AccountSettings state.
Located within the notifications/scam alerts area, this control navigates the user to the problematic programs listing to review current scam or non-paying programs.
Provides contextual navigation buttons such as a control to return to the main dashboard after saving settings.
Displays success or error messages resulting from updateAccountSettings and toggleScamAlertsSubscription operations, helping users confirm that their changes have been applied.

Global search input available in the page header. Uses searchSite to validate the search query or prefetch results and then navigates to the search results page with the query encoded in URL parameters.

layout.grid_system: Implicit single-column fluid layout, likely based on simple block elements or a basic HTML table for some parts rather than a formal grid system.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a single, top-to-bottom, left-aligned column of stacked sections. At the very top sits the forum-style page header strip with the page title on the left and the header search form on the right, all on a plain white background. Below that, the main "Settings" content appears as one large bordered box that spans most of the page width. Inside this box, a thin horizontal tab navigation row sits at the top, and directly under it the active settings form (dashboard, currency, or notifications) fills the bulk of the container. Within each form, fields are arranged in simple vertical stacks of label–control rows, with occasional inline elements (toggles, links, buttons) on the same line.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a single, top-to-bottom, left-aligned column of stacked sections. At the very top sits the forum-style page header strip with the page title on the left and the header search form on the right, all on a plain white background. Below that, the main "Settings" content appears as one large bordered box that spans most of the page width. Inside this box, a thin horizontal tab navigation row sits at the top, and directly under it the active settings form (dashboard, currency, or notifications) fills the bulk of the container. Within each form, fields are arranged in simple vertical stacks of label–control rows, with occasional inline elements (toggles, links, buttons) on the same line.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Horizontal space is mostly dedicated to a single central column: the main settings box occupies roughly 80–90% of the viewport width, centered with modest left/right gutters of about 16–24px. Vertically, the header strip and search form take up minimal height, just enough for a single line of text and controls. The largest share of vertical space is given to the bordered settings container, which grows with the form content. Inside that container, the tab navigation strip takes only one short line; the rest of the interior height is filled by dense rows of form fields, toggles, and the bottom button row and feedback line.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content is tightly packed to mirror an old-school forum: small line-height, minimal vertical gaps between rows (around 4–8px), and lean padding around container edges (about 8–12px inside the bordered box). Inputs and buttons have very small internal padding (roughly 2–4px) and align closely with their labels. There is little decorative whitespace; gaps exist primarily to keep lines distinct, not to create airy sections. The overall feel is compact and utilitarian, maximizing the number of visible settings on a single screen.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye starts at the top-left page title, then glances to the right where the search field sits on the same horizontal line. From there, the gaze drops into the main bordered settings box, immediately catching the tab navigation labels aligned along the top-left of the box. The active tab label leads straight down into its form: the reader scans each row from left to right (label to control to any inline helper text or links) and then moves down to the next row, forming a strong vertical reading rhythm. Within the notifications section, the scam alerts toggle and scam list link appear in-line or closely stacked, encouraging a left-to-right scan of that specific control before moving down to the bottom button strip and then the save feedback message beneath it.

layout.overall_description: The Settings page looks like a clean, early-2000s forum configuration screen: a plain white background with a simple text header at the top-left and a narrow search box with a submit button at the top-right, all on one slender horizontal line. Beneath this, occupying most of the viewport width and height, is a single large, light-gray bordered rectangle centered on the page with modest margins from the browser edges. Along the top inside edge of this box, a single-line text-based tab row lists the different settings areas as plain links or bold labels, separated by simple characters rather than styled tabs. The active settings area fills the rest of the box with a dense stack of text labels and basic HTML controls, each on its own line or in compact inline groups, with only small gaps between rows. Near the bottom-right of the box, a small cluster of plain HTML buttons lets the user save or cancel changes, followed immediately underneath by a one-line status message in smaller, gray text when settings are saved. The overall effect is purely functional, text-driven, and vertically linear: the eye flows from the top header down into the bordered module, follows the tabs to the active settings form, then descends steadily through each control line to the action buttons and feedback note at the bottom.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The settings layout acts as the main bordered container occupying roughly 80–90% of the viewport width, centered horizontally with even left and right margins of about 16–24px. It begins just below the header strip (title and header search) with a small vertical gap of about 8–12px and extends downward as far as the settings content requires. Visually, it is a simple rectangular box with a thin gray border and a white interior. Inside its top edge, there is around 8–12px of padding before the settings-tab-navigation strip starts, and similar padding around the sides and bottom where the forms and buttons sit. All inner components (tab navigation, active form, buttons, status feedback) are placed in a single vertical stack within this box, left-aligned with its inner padding edge, creating one cohesive, columnar block of settings content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, fluid-width container: it scales with the page width up to a reasonable max (e.g., around 960–1100px), always centered, and grows vertically with its contents without fixed height or scrolling regions inside.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The tab navigation appears as the first row inside the settings-layout box, touching its top inner padding. It is a single horizontal line of text labels (for example: "Dashboard", "Currency", "Notifications") aligned to the left. Each tab label is plain text or a simple link, separated by minimal spacing such as a single space, vertical bar, or hyphen, reflecting the utilitarian style. The active tab may be bolded or non-linked but remains on the same baseline as the others. The strip itself is only about one line tall, with approximately 4–8px of vertical padding above and below the text, and spans the full width of the container even though content is left-aligned. Directly below this strip, with a very small gap (4–8px), the active settings form begins, giving the impression of the tab row sitting like a heading for the content area underneath.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static inline row within the main container; it does not stick or float on scroll. Only tab styling state (active/inactive) changes; position remains fixed at the top of the settings-layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dashboard settings form occupies the main body area inside the settings-layout box immediately below the tab navigation when the "Dashboard" tab is active. It starts about 4–8px beneath the tab row and stretches almost the full interior width, leaving only the container’s 8–12px side padding on left and right. Fields are arranged as a vertical stack of rows; each row is a label on the left and a basic HTML control (select, checkbox, or text input) to its immediate right, on the same line, separated by a small 4–8px gap. Labels are left-aligned and align vertically along a visible label column, giving a neat column effect. Between field rows, there is only a small vertical gap of around 4–8px, preserving high density. Any helper text appears inline after the control, in smaller or gray text on the same line where possible, or directly on the line beneath with minimal indentation. The form content stops a short distance (about 12–16px) above the settings-navigation-buttons at the bottom of the container.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Normal block-level form that appears and disappears based on the active tab state; when visible it fills the central vertical space between the tab navigation above and the navigation buttons below.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The currency settings form occupies exactly the same position and footprint as the dashboard form but is shown instead when the "Currency" tab is active. It begins 4–8px below the tab navigation and extends downward in a similar dense vertical stack of label–control pairs. The default display currency selector is positioned within the top one or two rows, with the currency label on the left and a compact dropdown list to its immediate right, consuming only as much horizontal space as needed. If there are additional options like currency formatting or rounding, they appear as subsequent rows aligned with the same label column. Horizontal alignment and spacing mimic the other forms: labels left, controls directly adjacent, minimal inline spacing, and small gaps between rows, with the whole block stopping just above the bottom button group.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Normal block-level form; it replaces the dashboard and notification forms in the same central area when its tab is selected, inheriting the same fluid height behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The notification settings form also sits directly below the tab navigation, occupying the same central content area when the "Notifications" tab is active. Its rows list various notification preferences in a compact vertical series. Each row starts with a plain text label on the left (for example, "Email alerts", "In-site alerts", "Risk level threshold") followed by a basic control (checkbox, select, or text input) on the same line. The scam-alerts-toggle row is positioned in the upper-middle portion of this form, as one of the key options. All lines are left-aligned, with around 4–8px vertical spacing between rows and minimal horizontal separation between label and control. The form’s bottom edge remains a short distance above the settings-navigation-buttons, leaving a consistent 12–16px gap for visual breathing room within the box.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Normal block-level form that displays only when its corresponding tab is active, using the same vertical space between the tab row and the bottom button strip.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The scam alerts toggle appears as one dedicated row within the notification-settings-form, aligned with the other notification options. On this row, a short left-aligned label such as "Scam alerts" or "Scam & risk alerts" is placed flush with the form’s label column. Immediately to the right of the label, with a small 4–8px horizontal gap, sits a simple checkbox or on/off-style basic HTML control, followed by concise inline text explaining what the toggle does (for example, "Enable alerts for flagged HYIPs"), in the same line or breaking to a second line only if the viewport is narrow. If it wraps, the wrapped text aligns under the description text, not under the label, preserving a narrow label column on the left and a wider content column on the right. The overall row height is just slightly taller than a single text line, keeping this prominent setting compact but clearly visible.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static inline control within the notification form; it behaves like any other form row, with no floating or sticky behavior, but its position in the upper-middle of the form gives it importance.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The scam list link is positioned directly adjacent to the scam-alerts-toggle row to tie it contextually to that setting. It appears either on the same line, immediately after the scam alerts description text, or on the line directly below but indented so that it visually aligns with the start of the description (not with the label). In a typical layout, it would follow a small separator, such as a dash or parentheses – for example: "Enable alerts for flagged HYIPs – [View known scam list]" – echoing the hyphen-separated text style from the original program list. The link text is blue, inline-sized, and uses the same font size as the surrounding text. The horizontal space it occupies is minimal; it does not form a new block but reads like an inline helper link tightly coupled to the scam alerts toggle.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline text link anchored to the scam alerts row; it moves naturally with that row’s wrapping behavior and does not have independent positioning or fixed behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The navigation buttons appear as the final functional row inside the settings-layout box, just below whichever settings form is active. This row is separated from the last form field by a narrow vertical gap of about 8–12px. Within the row, one or two small HTML buttons (such as "Save Settings" and "Cancel") are aligned to the right edge of the container’s inner padding, matching the visual pattern of old-school form footers. Each button is compact, with minimal internal padding (about 2–4px vertically and 6–8px horizontally), and positioned on a single baseline with a small 4–8px gap between them. The left side of this row is mostly empty whitespace, emphasizing the buttons without adding decoration. Immediately below this row, the settings-save-feedback line appears, visually connected as the result of pressing these buttons.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block at the bottom of the main container; its position is anchored after the form content. It does not float; it shifts downward only as the form above grows taller.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The save feedback indicator is a single line of text directly beneath the settings-navigation-buttons row, separated from the buttons by a very small vertical gap of about 4–8px. It is left-aligned with the main content column inside the settings-layout (i.e., starting at the same left margin as the form labels), creating a subtle offset from the right-aligned buttons just above. The text is smaller or slightly lighter gray than the main body text to mark it as status information, e.g., "Settings saved successfully" or "Error saving settings". The line occupies the full width of the container but rarely wraps; it remains visually compact and does not add significant height unless a longer message is needed. When no feedback is present, this line may be invisible, leaving only a thin gap below the buttons before the container’s bottom padding and border.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static text line that appears or disappears based on form submission state; it does not reserve fixed height, simply pushing the bottom of the container down when visible.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The header search form is placed in the topmost page header area, horizontally aligned with the page title. The title text "Settings" sits at the top-left, and on the same horizontal baseline, the search form is positioned on the far right, separated by flexible whitespace between them. The search form consists of a small, plain text input box and a basic submit button to its immediate right, both using minimal padding of about 2–4px and appearing as default browser controls on a white background. This entire header strip spans nearly the full page width but is only one line tall, with about 8–12px of vertical padding above and below to keep text from touching the browser edges. A small vertical gap (around 8–12px) separates this header from the top of the main settings-layout box below, maintaining the compact stacked structure seen in old forum layouts.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and top-aligned within the page flow; it scrolls away with the rest of the content and does not stick to the top of the viewport.

name: Forum Index
title: Forum
description: Index page listing main forum categories and providing entry into HYIP-related discussions, with optional forum-focused search.

core_features: Fetch and display the list of top-level forum categories using getForumIndex
Show basic statistics per category (thread count, post count, last activity) based on ForumCategory data
Allow navigation into a specific forum category by clicking its name, passing categoryId via URL parameters
Provide a forum-focused search form that calls searchSite with user-entered keywords and forum-related type_filter
Optionally display a preview of forum thread search results on the index page when a search query is present in the URL
user_workflows:
Browse categories workflow:
1) User navigates to forum.html (e.g., via header navigation or direct link).
2) On page init, the forum-index-loader component calls getForumIndex.
3) The forum-category-table component renders the returned ForumCategory list, including names, descriptions, and stats.
4) User clicks a category name in the table.
5) Browser navigates to forum_category.html with categoryId set to the selected ForumCategory.id (e.g., forum_category.html?categoryId={id}).

Forum search workflow (from Forum Index):
1) User enters a search term into the forum-global-search-form on forum.html.
2) User optionally chooses a forum-only filter or leaves default as configured by the app.
3) On submit, the form updates the forum.html URL with parameters for the query (e.g., ?q={userQuery}) and optional type_filter and page.
4) The forum-index-loader detects the updated URL and calls searchSite with the query, a forum-related type_filter value, and current page/page_size.
5) The forum-search-results-preview component displays a paginated list of matching ForumThread records.
6) User can click a specific search result to navigate (externally defined) to the thread’s detail page, or click a "view all results" control that leads to a dedicated search results page URL including the same query parameters.

Search pagination workflow (within Forum Index preview):
1) When search results are visible on forum.html and the user changes the page (e.g., clicks next or a page number) in the forum-search-results-preview component, the component updates the URL search_page parameter.
2) The forum-index-loader re-calls searchSite with the existing query, type_filter, and the new page value.
3) The forum-search-results-preview re-renders using the updated forum_thread_results from searchSite.
interactions: On page load (forum.html without or with URL parameters), the forum-index-loader component reads URL parameters and calls getForumIndex. If a search query parameter is present, it also calls searchSite.
When the user types into the search input of forum-global-search-form, the component updates its internal query state but does not call any interface until submit.

On search form submit, forum-global-search-form prevents default browser submission, updates the browser URL with the current query, type_filter, page, and page_size parameters, and triggers a re-initialization of the forum-index-loader logic.

When the user clicks a forum category row or name in forum-category-table, the component constructs a URL for forum_category.html including categoryId from the bound ForumCategory.id and navigates there.
When the user interacts with pagination controls in forum-search-results-preview, the component updates the search_page parameter in the URL and triggers a new searchSite call via forum-index-loader.

When the user clicks a specific forum thread search result in forum-search-results-preview, the component navigates to the thread’s page URL with appropriate thread identifier parameters (defined outside this page’s scope).
page_functionality.state_logic:
The page state is fully derived from the URL of forum.html. The following URL parameters are used:
- q: Optional text search query. If present and not empty, forum-index-loader calls searchSite with this value as the query parameter; forum-search-results-preview is displayed.
- search_type_filter: Optional type_filter value to restrict searchSite to forum-related content (for example, to forum threads only). If present, it is passed directly to searchSite as type_filter. If absent, the page uses whatever default server-side handling is in place (no client-side hardcoded default).
- search_page: Optional current results page for searchSite. If present and valid, it is passed to searchSite as the page parameter; otherwise the initial page is determined by backend behavior. Pagination controls in forum-search-results-preview update this parameter.
- search_page_size: Optional page_size for searchSite. If present, it is passed as page_size; otherwise the backend default is used.

State behavior:
- If q is absent or empty, only the forum categories list is shown (from getForumIndex); searchSite is not called.
- If q is present, both the categories list and the search results preview can be displayed. The current values of search_type_filter, search_page, and search_page_size are taken from the URL and passed through to searchSite.
- Any user interaction that changes search-related state (new query, different filter, or pagination) results in updating the URL parameters, which then drive subsequent calls to searchSite. No local storage or in-memory-only state is relied upon for restoring page state on reload; reloading the page with the same URL parameters will reproduce the same visible state.

components.functionality: Coordinates data fetching for the Forum Index page by reading URL parameters, calling getForumIndex to load forum categories, and conditionally calling searchSite when a search query is present.

Captures user search input and search-related controls (such as a forum-only filter and optional page size) and encodes them into URL parameters for forum.html. Triggers searchSite indirectly by causing the forum-index-loader to run when URL changes.

Displays the list of top-level forum categories returned from getForumIndex, including each category’s name, optional description, thread_count, post_count, and last_activity_at. Provides clickable navigation to the corresponding forum_category.html page.

When a search query is present, shows a paginated list of forum thread results derived from searchSite’s forum_thread_results along with total_forum_thread_results. Presents basic thread information and navigation to thread pages, as well as pagination controls and an optional link to a dedicated search results page.

layout.grid_system: Implicit single-column fluid layout, likely based on simple block elements or a basic HTML table for some parts rather than a formal grid system.
layout.layout_pattern: All components sit in a single, fluid, top-to-bottom column aligned to the left, with no multi-column layout. The page content is wrapped in a simple block container with small left/right margins from the browser edge. Within this column, components are stacked in this order: (1) forum-index-loader (only visible while loading), (2) forum-global-search-form, (3) forum-search-results-preview (when applicable), (4) forum-category-table. Each component is a full-width block inside the main content column, echoing the header > login strip > breadcrumb > main list stacking from the mockup.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: All components sit in a single, fluid, top-to-bottom column aligned to the left, with no multi-column layout. The page content is wrapped in a simple block container with small left/right margins from the browser edge. Within this column, components are stacked in this order: (1) forum-index-loader (only visible while loading), (2) forum-global-search-form, (3) forum-search-results-preview (when applicable), (4) forum-category-table. Each component is a full-width block inside the main content column, echoing the header > login strip > breadcrumb > main list stacking from the mockup.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The forum-category-table is the dominant element, occupying the majority of vertical space and nearly the full width of the content column. The search form and search results preview are compact strips at the top, similar in height to the login strip and breadcrumb from the mockup, with small vertical gaps (about 8–12px) between them. Side margins around the main content column are modest (about 16–24px), but inside each box padding is minimal (about 8–12px) to keep the layout dense.
layout_strategies.content_density: Text and controls are tightly packed: line-height is relatively small and vertical padding around rows or list items is limited to roughly 4–8px. Inputs and buttons use only 2–4px internal padding. The goal is to fit more rows (categories or search results) into the visible area, mirroring the high information density of an old-school forum index. There is enough spacing to keep lines distinct, but no decorative whitespace or large breathing areas.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Reading flow is strictly vertical: users start at the page title (outside the defined components), then immediately encounter the search strip, optional search results preview, and finally move into the main category list. Within each horizontal strip, the eye moves left-to-right: labels to inputs in the search form, and in lists from left identifiers (e.g., category name or thread title) toward secondary metadata (e.g., counts) on the right. The primary scanning pattern is straight down the page, with no visual interruptions or sidebars.

layout.overall_description: Imagine a simple, white page that looks like an early 2000s forum: no logos, no icons, just text, plain links, and basic form controls. The main content column starts slightly inset from the browser edges, with about a 16–24px margin on the left and right. At the top of this column you first see a compact search strip—just a line of label text, a single-line text field, maybe a dropdown, and a basic submit button, all inline and left-aligned. Directly beneath it (when there are results) is a shallow bordered box containing a tight, text-only preview list of matching threads, each on its own line. Below that, taking up most of the screen height, is a larger bordered box: the forum-category-table. It spans almost the entire width of the content column and contains tightly stacked rows listing the forum's main categories and related stats. If data is loading, a small plain-text 'Loading…' line appears just above the search strip or above the main boxes, then disappears once content is ready. Overall, the page feels compact and utilitarian, with a strong top-to-bottom reading flow and nearly all available vertical space devoted to lists of forum information.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The forum-index-loader is a very small, unobtrusive text line positioned at the top of the main content column, immediately below any global page title or login/breadcrumb elements, and just above the forum-global-search-form. It spans the full width of the content column but typically contains only a short status phrase such as 'Loading forum index…'. The text is left-aligned and uses the same basic body type as the rest of the page, potentially in a slightly lighter gray to indicate secondary importance. There is minimal vertical padding—about 4px above and below—so that this line reads as a slim notification strip rather than a blocky banner. When inactive, this component collapses entirely, giving its vertical space back so that the search form sits directly beneath the header/breadcrumb area. There is a small vertical gap of roughly 8px between this loader line (when visible) and the search form that follows, maintaining the compact stacking pattern from the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The loader behaves as a normal block element in the document flow: it appears and disappears without affecting the overall layout rules, simply pushing subsequent components down when visible. It is not fixed or sticky and does not overlay other content; it only occupies a single line’s height at the top of the content column when active.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The forum-global-search-form is rendered as a single horizontal strip near the top of the main content column, echoing the login strip style from the mockup. It sits directly below the loader (if visible) or, otherwise, directly below the page’s breadcrumb/title region, with a small vertical gap of around 8–12px. The form is left-aligned and spans nearly the full width of the content column, but its controls occupy only the left half to two-thirds, leaving empty white space to the right. The components of the form—plain text label (e.g., 'Search:'), a single-line text input, an optional dropdown for scope (e.g., 'in forums'), and a basic submit button—are arranged inline in a single row, with tiny horizontal gaps (about 4–8px) between them. Inputs and buttons have minimal internal padding (around 2–4px) and use default browser styling to maintain the utilitarian, early-web feel. There is about 8px of vertical padding within this strip, ensuring the text and controls don’t feel cramped against the top or bottom edge. A small 8px gap separates this form from the component below (search-results-preview, when visible, or the forum-category-table otherwise), preserving a tight but structured vertical rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The search form is a standard block flowing with the document; it does not stick to the top of the viewport. On narrower viewports, its inline elements may naturally wrap to a second line, but it remains a compact strip with no special floating or fixed behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The forum-category-table is the dominant content block on the page, visually analogous to the large bordered list box in the mockup. It appears below the search area: if search-results-preview is present, the table sits directly beneath it with a small 8–12px vertical gap; if not, the table sits just below the search form. The table is contained in a simple rectangular box that spans nearly 100% of the main content column width, inset from the browser edge by about 16–24px via the page margin. The box has a thin, 1px light-gray border on all sides and a plain white background. Inside this box, content is dense and text-oriented: there may be a header row at the top with bold labels (e.g., 'Category', 'Topics', 'Posts', 'Last Post'), followed by multiple rows or sections where each forum category occupies one row. Category names appear as blue, underlined links aligned to the left, with descriptions and numeric stats either in adjacent columns or as smaller gray text to the right. Row height is tight: each row has around 4–8px of vertical padding, and line-height is modest to allow many rows to fit in the viewport. There is roughly 8–12px of inner padding from the box border to the first and last rows on all sides, mirroring the main list container in the mockup. Vertically, this box expands as needed with content and generally occupies most of the page’s visible height, making it the primary focus of the index.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The forum-category-table is a regular block-level element that stretches fluidly with the content column width. It scrolls with the page; no fixed headers or sticky columns are used. If table markup is employed, it retains a classic HTML forum look, with straightforward cell borders or subtle row separators, staying consistent with the old-school aesthetic.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The forum-search-results-preview sits between the search form and the main category table, acting as an optional, compact list that appears only after a search is performed. When present, it is rendered as a bordered rectangular box spanning nearly the full width of the content column, aligned left and sharing the same left/right margins as the forum-category-table. There is a small 8–12px gap between the bottom of the search form and the top of this preview box, and another similar gap between the bottom of this box and the top of the forum-category-table, keeping all elements tightly stacked but clearly separated. The box uses a thin 1px light-gray border and a white background, with about 8–12px of internal padding. Inside, search results are presented as a vertical list—either as a simple ordered or unordered list or as single-row entries. Each result is one line of text, starting with the thread or program title as a blue underlined link aligned to the left, optionally followed by a hyphen and a secondary blue link for the forum section or program domain, and then smaller gray text in parentheses for match context or reply counts. Lines are tightly spaced with about 4–8px vertical padding, echoing the list density from the mockup. The preview is not intended to be very tall: it might show only the first few matches, keeping the user aware of search hits while still drawing the main attention downward into the full category-table below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The search-results-preview participates in normal document flow and only occupies space when there are results to show; otherwise it is omitted, allowing the forum-category-table to move up directly beneath the search form. It does not float or stick and scrolls naturally with the rest of the page content.

name: Forum Category
title: Forum Category
description: Displays all threads within a selected forum category (e.g. New HYIPs), with sorting, pagination, navigation to individual threads, and an action to create a new thread. Includes a global search interaction across programs and forum threads.

core_features: Load and display the current forum category metadata (name, description, stats) using getForumCategoryThreads(categoryId, sort_by, page, page_size).
Display a paginated list of threads for the given category, including title, optional prefix, author, reply count, and last activity time, using the threads returned by getForumCategoryThreads.
Provide sorting controls for threads (e.g., by newest, most replied, last post) that update the sort_by URL parameter and reload data via getForumCategoryThreads.
Provide pagination controls that update the page and page_size URL parameters and reload data via getForumCategoryThreads.
Allow navigation to an individual thread page when a thread title is clicked, passing threadId in the URL.
Allow creation of a new thread in the current category via a "New Thread" action that navigates to forum_new_thread.html with categoryId in the URL.

Provide a global search form that uses searchSite(query, type_filter, page, page_size) to show inline preview results (programs and forum threads) and supports navigation to detailed results when a preview item is clicked.
user_workflows:
View threads in a category:
1. User navigates to forum_category.html with a categoryId URL parameter.
2. Page reads categoryId, sort_by, page, and page_size from the URL.
3. Page calls getForumCategoryThreads with these parameters.
4. Category header displays the returned category information.
5. Thread list displays the returned threads and their key metadata.
6. Pagination controls display based on the returned pagination object.

Change thread sorting:
1. User interacts with the sorting control (e.g., selects a different sort option).
2. Page updates the sort_by parameter in the URL (and optionally resets page to the first page) using client-side routing.
3. Page re-calls getForumCategoryThreads with the updated sort_by (and page) values.
4. Thread list and pagination re-render with the new order.

Navigate between pages of threads:
1. User clicks a pagination control (e.g., a specific page number or next/previous).
2. Page updates the page parameter (and optionally page_size if changed) in the URL.
3. Page calls getForumCategoryThreads with the updated page (and page_size) values.
4. Thread list updates to show the corresponding slice of threads.

Open a specific thread:
1. User clicks on a thread title or main thread row in the list.
2. Page reads the thread.id from the selected item.
3. Page navigates to forum_thread.html, adding threadId=selectedThreadId to the URL.

Create a new thread in this category:
1. User clicks the "New Thread" button in the category header.
2. Page reads the current category.id from the loaded category object.
3. Page navigates to forum_new_thread.html with categoryId set to this value in the URL.

Navigate back to Forum Index:
1. User clicks the breadcrumb link labeled for the forum index.
2. Page navigates to forum.html without additional parameters (or as defined by the forum index page).

Use global search from this page:
1. User types a search term into the global search input.
2. On submit or after a defined interaction (e.g., pressing Enter or clicking a search button), the page calls searchSite with query, and optionally type_filter, page, and page_size derived from the current search state.
3. The page displays inline preview results for both program_results and forum_thread_results.
4. User may click a program result to navigate to that program’s detail page (handled by the parent site navigation) or click a forum thread result to navigate to forum_thread.html with threadId set to the selected thread’s id.
interactions:
Sorting interactions:
- Change sort option: User selects a different sort criterion in the sorting control. Event handler updates the sort_by URL parameter, optionally adjusts page to the first page, and triggers a new getForumCategoryThreads call.
- Clear or reset sort: If provided, a reset option removes or resets the sort_by parameter in the URL and re-fetches threads.

Pagination interactions:
- Click specific page number: Updates the page URL parameter to the selected page and calls getForumCategoryThreads.
- Click next/previous: Computes the next/previous page from the pagination data, updates the page URL parameter, and calls getForumCategoryThreads.
- Change items per page (if exposed): Updates the page_size URL parameter (and typically page to the first page), then calls getForumCategoryThreads.

Thread list interactions:
- Click thread title/row: Navigates to forum_thread.html?threadId={thread.id}.
- Hover or focus a thread item: May reveal additional metadata (purely presentational; no interface calls).
- Indicator display for locked or subscribed threads: Uses is_locked and is_subscribed from ForumThread for passive display without modifying any data.

Category header and navigation interactions:
- Click "New Thread" button: Navigates to forum_new_thread.html?categoryId={category.id}.
- Click breadcrumb for forum index: Navigates to forum.html.
- Click breadcrumb for current category (if present): Navigates to forum_category.html with the same categoryId but may clear sort_by/page/page_size parameters if designed as a reset.

Global search interactions:
- Enter query text: Binds the input value to a local query state.
- Submit search: Triggers an onSubmit handler which calls searchSite with the current query and other search parameters, then displays inline preview results within this page.
- Click program result in search preview: Navigates to the program detail page URL with the appropriate program identifier (handled by global site routing outside this page’s interfaces).
- Click forum thread result in search preview: Navigates to forum_thread.html?threadId={resultThread.id}.
- Dismiss search preview: User action (e.g., clicking outside or closing a panel) hides preview results without further API calls.

Initial and subsequent data loading:
- On initial page load or when URL parameters change (categoryId, sort_by, page, page_size), a controller-level effect detects the change and triggers a new getForumCategoryThreads call. All category and thread-related components re-bind to the returned data.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The page state is fully derived from URL parameters and server responses; no localStorage or other persistent client storage is used.

URL Parameters:
- categoryId (required to display a category):
  - Determines which ForumCategory and associated ForumThread items are loaded.
  - On page load, categoryId is read from the URL. If it changes (e.g., user navigates programmatically), the page re-calls getForumCategoryThreads with the new categoryId.

- sort_by (optional):
  - Controls the ordering of threads returned by getForumCategoryThreads.
  - When the user changes sorting, the onChange handler updates sort_by in the URL (either by adding, replacing, or removing this parameter depending on the selected option) and triggers a re-fetch.
  - If sort_by is absent, the request is made without this parameter, allowing server-side defaults or behavior defined by getForumCategoryThreads.

- page (optional):
  - Controls which page of results is requested from getForumCategoryThreads.
  - On pagination interactions, the handler updates page in the URL and triggers a re-fetch.
  - If page is absent, the request is made without this parameter; the backend determines the default page.

- page_size (optional):
  - Controls how many threads are requested per page in getForumCategoryThreads.
  - If the UI exposes a page-size selector, changes update page_size in the URL and optionally reset page, then re-fetch.
  - If page_size is absent, the backend chooses its default page size.

Derived State:
- The category summary (ForumCategory) and thread list (array of ForumThread) are taken directly from the getForumCategoryThreads response and are not cached beyond the page lifecycle.
- Pagination UI state (current page, total pages, disabled state of next/previous) is derived from the pagination object returned by getForumCategoryThreads.
- Search-related state (query, optional type_filter, search preview results) is transient in component state and is not written to the URL on this page unless explicitly designed to do so by higher-level navigation.

URL-driven Re-rendering:
- Any change to categoryId, sort_by, page, or page_size in the URL leads to a new call to getForumCategoryThreads with the current URL parameter values.
- Components that depend on category, threads, or pagination automatically re-bind to the newest response and re-render.

components.functionality: Displays a breadcrumb trail allowing the user to navigate back to the forum index or reset to the current category view.
Shows the selected forum category’s name, optional description, and aggregate stats, and provides the 'New Thread' action for this category.
Allows the user to choose how threads are ordered (e.g., newest, most replied, last updated) and updates the sort_by URL parameter to trigger reloading of threads.

Displays the list of ForumThread items for the current category, including key metadata such as title, prefix, author, reply count, and last activity time. Handles navigation to an individual thread when clicked.

Shows pagination controls based on the pagination object returned by getForumCategoryThreads and updates the page and page_size URL parameters when the user navigates between pages or changes page size.

Central controller that reads URL parameters (categoryId, sort_by, page, page_size), calls getForumCategoryThreads, and distributes the resulting category, threads, and pagination data to other components.
Captures a user’s search query and triggers searchSite to fetch cross-site results (programs and forum threads) for inline preview.
Displays inline preview results from searchSite, including both program_results and forum_thread_results, and allows navigation to the appropriate detail pages when a result is clicked.

layout.grid_system: Implicit single-column fluid layout, likely based on simple block elements or a basic HTML table for some parts rather than a formal grid system.
layout.layout_pattern: Use a strictly linear, top-to-bottom single-column flow. At the very top of the main content area, place a horizontal strip with the category header text aligned to the left and the global search form aligned to the right on the same baseline, mimicking the title + login row from the mockup. Directly below that, a full-width breadcrumb line. Beneath the breadcrumb, a narrow full-width strip for thread sorting controls. The main content is a large bordered box containing the thread list, with a lightweight loading indicator at the top of that box and pagination controls at the bottom of it. Search preview results, when visible, appear directly under the global search form, aligned to it, without altering the core vertical stacking of the page.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Use a strictly linear, top-to-bottom single-column flow. At the very top of the main content area, place a horizontal strip with the category header text aligned to the left and the global search form aligned to the right on the same baseline, mimicking the title + login row from the mockup. Directly below that, a full-width breadcrumb line. Beneath the breadcrumb, a narrow full-width strip for thread sorting controls. The main content is a large bordered box containing the thread list, with a lightweight loading indicator at the top of that box and pagination controls at the bottom of it. Search preview results, when visible, appear directly under the global search form, aligned to it, without altering the core vertical stacking of the page.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Reserve modest left and right margins from the browser edge (around 16–24px), with all content blocks (header/search row, breadcrumb, sorting controls, main list box) spanning nearly the full content width. Allocate most vertical space to the bordered thread list container, which stretches down the page and dominates the viewport. The top strips (header + search, breadcrumb, sorting) are compact bands with minimal height, just enough for a single line of text and controls, so that the thread list can occupy roughly 70–80% of the vertical viewport on typical screens.
layout_strategies.content_density: Maintain high information density throughout. Use tight vertical spacing between rows (around 4–8px gaps between stacked sections) and small internal padding (8–12px) inside the main thread list box. Individual thread rows are single lines (or minimal wrap) with small line-height and very little extra vertical padding, allowing many threads to be visible at once. Controls like inputs and buttons use minimal internal padding (2–4px), aligning with a utilitarian early-2000s web aesthetic.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Guide the eye from the top-left category title across to the right-aligned global search form, then down in a straight line through the breadcrumb, sorting strip, and into the large thread box. Within each thread row, the eye moves left to right: ordinal number, blue program title link, hyphen, blue domain link, then a lighter gray reply-count note in parentheses at the far right or near the end of the line. Search preview results appear directly under the search field so that when typing, attention shifts briefly to the top-right, but the dominant vertical reading path remains focused on scanning down the thread list.

layout.overall_description: The page resembles a classic early-2000s forum category view: a white background with simple black text, blue links, and subtle gray borders. All content sits inside a single, fluid central column with about 16–24px of whitespace at the left and right edges of the browser. At the very top of this column, a narrow horizontal bar contains the bold category title on the far left and, on the same line, a plain text-driven global search form aligned to the right. Directly beneath this, a single-line breadcrumb spans the width, followed by another narrow strip for sorting controls. Below these compact bands, a large rectangular box with a thin gray border and 8–12px inner padding fills most of the vertical space; inside it, a dense vertical list of threads is numbered and link-heavy, each line tightly spaced to show many entries at once. A lightweight loading message area sits at the top of this box (appearing when data is loading), and pagination text links align to the bottom-right inside or directly below the box. When the user types in the global search field, a simple, text-only preview panel drops down directly under the search input, occupying a small stacked area in the top-right while leaving the main vertical flow of the page unchanged. The overall effect is utilitarian and content-first, with a strong top-to-bottom reading rhythm and no decorative distractions.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The breadcrumb trail is positioned directly below the top header/search strip, spanning nearly the full content width. It is a single horizontal line of small-to-regular sized text, left-aligned, starting about 16–24px from the left browser edge, with roughly 4–8px of vertical space above and below it. There is no special box or background; it sits directly on the white background. The breadcrumb text flows inline (e.g., Home > Forum > New HYIPs) with minimal spacing between links and separators, maintaining the compact, utilitarian feel. The right side of this line remains mostly empty, as the breadcrumb does not extend all the way across unless the path is long. It acts as a clear visual divider between the header/search row above and the sorting controls below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows in the normal document flow as a full-width text row; scrolls off the screen with the rest of the content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The category header forms the left side of the very top content strip. It is placed at the top-left of the main content column, starting approximately 16–24px from the left browser edge and a similar margin from the top. The main category title (e.g., 'New HYIPs') appears as a single line of bold, slightly larger black text, aligned to the left. If there is a short description, it appears directly under the title as a second line in normal-weight text with slightly smaller font size, but still within the same left-aligned block. Horizontally, this header block occupies roughly the left 50–60% of the available width; the remaining right 40–50% is visually shared with the global search form on the same top row. Vertical height is kept compact, with about 4–8px of space above and below the text block, reinforcing the tight, information-dense style.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level element at the top of the content column; participates in normal flow, sharing a row with the global search form through simple inline or floated layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The thread sorting controls appear as a thin horizontal strip immediately below the breadcrumb line, separated by about 4–8px of vertical space. They span the full content width but occupy minimal vertical height, just enough for a single line of text and basic form controls. On the left side, there might be a label such as 'Sort by:' followed by a basic HTML select dropdown (with default browser styling) or a small set of radio buttons/links, all arranged in a single row. The controls are left-aligned with the breadcrumb and category header above, starting at the same left margin (around 16–24px from the browser edge). The right side of this strip remains mostly empty, keeping the appearance clean and uncluttered. The strip does not have its own border or background; it visually blends into the page, acting as a functional seam between the breadcrumb and the large thread list box below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline/block elements laid out in normal flow, with no sticky behavior; they scroll away with the rest of the content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The thread list is contained within a large rectangular box that begins directly below the sorting controls, with about 8–12px of vertical separation. This box spans nearly the full content width, starting around 16–24px from the left and ending at a similar margin on the right, and it uses a thin gray border with a white background. Inside, there is 8–12px of padding on all sides. The threads themselves are arranged in a single ordered list running top to bottom, each item occupying one line or minimally wrapping onto two lines if necessary. Each line starts with an ordinal number on the far left, followed by a small amount of horizontal space, then a blue program name link, a hyphen, and a blue domain link. At the end of the line, or slightly offset after the domain, a smaller, gray, parenthetical note shows the number of replies (e.g., '(12 replies)'). The text is tightly spaced, with minimal vertical padding between list items (around 2–4px) and relatively small line-height, allowing many threads to be visible at once. The overall block dominates the central and lower portion of the page, filling most of the viewport vertically and continuing downward as the user scrolls.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A standard block-level container in normal document flow that expands vertically with the number of threads; it scrolls normally with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The pagination controls are placed at the bottom of the thread list area. They sit either inside the bordered thread list box, aligned to the bottom-right corner, or directly below the box with only 4–8px of vertical separation, depending on implementation; in both cases they appear visually attached to the thread list. The controls are simple text-based links (e.g., 'Previous  1  2  3  Next'), aligned to the right edge of the content column, using the same basic font as the rest of the page. There is no special background; they rest on white, with perhaps a slight top padding (4–8px) separating them from the last thread row. The pagination line is compact, occupying only a single row in height, ensuring that it does not visually compete with the thread list itself.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Part of the normal document flow. It appears immediately after the thread list content and moves down as more threads are added; it scrolls with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The category data loader is represented visually as a very lightweight status area at the top of the thread list box. Inside the bordered thread list container, directly under its top padding, a single line of small, gray text appears when data is loading (e.g., 'Loading threads…' or 'Refreshing list…'). This status line is left-aligned, starting at the same left margin as the thread numbers, and separated from the first thread row by roughly 4–8px of vertical space. When no loading is occurring, this line is hidden entirely, and the first thread row appears directly under the top padding of the box. There are no special spinners or graphics—only text—keeping with the minimal, old-school look.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally rendered inline block within the top of the thread list container; appears and disappears without changing the overall structure, only shifting thread rows up or down by one line.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The global search form occupies the top-right portion of the same horizontal strip as the category header. It is aligned on the same vertical baseline as the category title. Horizontally, it begins around the midpoint of the content width and extends toward the right margin, leaving a small gap (about 16–24px) from the right browser edge. The form is arranged in a single compact row: a small text label like 'Search:' immediately followed by a plain HTML text input field and a small default-styled submit button. The input is moderately wide, but not oversized (roughly 30–40% of the content width), and both input and button have minimal internal padding (2–4px) and default browser borders. The entire search form sits flush against the top of the content column, with about 4–8px of vertical whitespace above and below, maintaining the dense, utilitarian header band.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Right-aligned inline/form element sharing a row with the category header. It flows with the document and scrolls off-screen with the rest of the content. Acts as the anchor for the search preview panel positioned directly beneath it.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The search preview results panel appears directly below the global search input, aligned to its left edge and matching its width or slightly wider so that it accommodates result lines comfortably. Visually, it sits in the top-right region of the page, just underneath the header/search strip, but when open it overlays the content immediately below (such as part of the breadcrumb or the upper area of the page), similar to a basic dropdown. The panel is a simple white rectangle with a thin gray border, containing a vertical list of text-only result rows. Each row is a single line of small text with blue links and minimal vertical padding (2–4px). There is little to no extra whitespace inside beyond a small (4–8px) padding around the edges. When the user is not typing or when there are no preview results, this panel is hidden entirely, and the underlying breadcrumb and content appear as normal. The panel never pushes the main layout down; it simply overlaps it in a minimal, utilitarian manner.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally displayed overlay, positioned directly under and anchored to the global search form (e.g., absolutely positioned relative to the search form container). It floats above the normal document flow when visible but does not remain fixed when the page scrolls; if open, it scrolls away along with the header area.

name: Forum Thread
title: Forum Thread
description: Displays a single forum thread with its posts, allows users to read and reply to the discussion, change post sorting and pagination, and subscribe or unsubscribe to thread updates.

core_features: Load and display a forum thread and its posts using getForumThreadView based on URL parameters (threadId, page, page_size, sort_direction).
Show thread metadata (title, prefix, author, creation date, reply count, lock status, subscription status).
Display posts in the thread in the configured sort order with pagination controls.
Allow users to submit replies to the thread using replyToForumThread (disabled when the thread is locked).
Allow users to subscribe or unsubscribe to thread updates using toggleThreadSubscription and reflect the current subscription state.
Provide navigation to poster profiles (user_profile.html) via username/avatar clicks.
Provide navigation back to the thread’s category (forum_category.html) via breadcrumbs.
Provide a global search form that uses searchSite to search across HYIP programs and forum threads.
user_workflows: View thread and posts: 1) User navigates to forum_thread.html with threadId (and optional page, page_size, sort_direction) in the URL. 2) On load, the page calls getForumThreadView with these parameters. 3) The thread header, actions bar, and post list render from the response. 4) Pagination state is shown using the returned pagination object.

Change post page: 1) User clicks a page number or next/previous in the pagination controls. 2) The page updates the URL parameters page (and optionally page_size if changed). 3) The page calls getForumThreadView with the new parameters. 4) The post list and pagination controls update based on the new response.

Change sort order: 1) User selects a sort direction (e.g., oldest first or newest first) in the sort control. 2) The page updates the sort_direction parameter in the URL and resets page to the first page. 3) The page calls getForumThreadView with the updated parameters. 4) Posts are re-rendered in the new order using the returned posts array.

Reply to thread: 1) User scrolls to the reply form or clicks a Reply button that focuses it. 2) User types a message body and submits the form. 3) The page calls replyToForumThread with threadId from the URL and the entered body. 4) On success, the new ForumPost from the response is either appended to the current list (if it belongs to the current pagination window) or the page updates the URL (e.g., to the appropriate page) and calls getForumThreadView again to refresh the view.

Subscribe or unsubscribe from thread: 1) User clicks the subscribe/unsubscribe control in the thread actions bar. 2) The page determines the desired subscribe boolean (opposite of the current thread.is_subscribed) and calls toggleThreadSubscription with threadId and subscribe. 3) On success, the component updates the locally displayed subscription state using the returned is_subscribed value. 4) The control’s label and icon update accordingly.
Navigate to a poster’s profile: 1) User clicks a post author’s username or avatar. 2) The page navigates to user_profile.html with profileId set to post.author_profile_id in the URL parameters.

Navigate to the thread category: 1) User clicks the category name in the breadcrumb navigation. 2) The page navigates to forum_category.html with categoryId set to thread.category_id in the URL parameters.

Search across programs and threads: 1) User enters a keyword in the global search input in the header. 2) On submit, the page calls searchSite with query and pagination parameters (e.g., page and page_size) and optional type_filter. 3) The page can either show an inline summary of results (e.g., counts for programs and threads) or navigate to search_results.html with query and other search state encoded as URL parameters; the search results page then uses searchSite to display full results.
interactions: On initial page load or when URL parameters (threadId, page, page_size, sort_direction) change, call getForumThreadView and bind the returned ForumThread, ForumPost list, and pagination object to the relevant components.
Clicking a breadcrumb link triggers navigation to forum_category.html with categoryId=thread.category_id in the query string.
Clicking a post author’s username or avatar triggers navigation to user_profile.html with profileId=post.author_profile_id in the query string.
Changing the sort order control updates the sort_direction URL parameter, optionally resets page to the first page, and triggers a new getForumThreadView call.
Clicking a pagination control updates the page (and optionally page_size) URL parameters and triggers a new getForumThreadView call.

Submitting the reply form triggers replyToForumThread with threadId and body; on success, the new post is integrated into the visible post list or the page is reloaded via getForumThreadView. If thread.is_locked is true, the reply form is disabled and cannot be submitted.
Clicking the subscription toggle control triggers toggleThreadSubscription; the UI updates based on the returned is_subscribed value.
Submitting the global search form calls searchSite with the current query and then either displays inline results or navigates to search_results.html with the query encoded in the URL.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page state is controlled via URL parameters only. threadId is required; without it the page cannot call getForumThreadView and should show an error state instead of thread content. On load, the page reads threadId, page, page_size, and sort_direction from the URL and passes them to getForumThreadView. If page is missing or invalid, the implementation should treat it as the first page. If page_size is missing or invalid, the implementation should use a default internal value and, when user changes it via pagination controls, update page_size in the URL. If sort_direction is missing, the implementation should use an internal default and only persist sort_direction when the user changes it. Every time the user changes pagination or sort controls, the URL parameters are updated and a fresh getForumThreadView call is made, ensuring the URL always reflects the current view and can be shared or refreshed. The replyToForumThread and toggleThreadSubscription interfaces do not modify URL parameters directly; they update server state, and the page either updates its in-memory state using the response (post or is_subscribed) or optionally refreshes the thread view via getForumThreadView while keeping URL parameters unchanged. The global search form maintains its query in the search input control; on submit it can add or update a query parameter in the URL for search_results.html, but it does not modify the forum_thread.html URL beyond navigation away from the page.

components.functionality: Fetches the thread and its posts from the server whenever threadId, page, page_size, or sort_direction in the URL change, and exposes the loaded ForumThread, posts array, and pagination object to child components.
event_handlers: onLoad(threadId, page, page_size, sort_direction) -> calls getForumThreadView and stores thread, posts, pagination in page state.
onUrlChange(threadId, page, page_size, sort_direction) -> calls getForumThreadView again and updates the bound data.

components.functionality: Displays navigation hierarchy for the forum, including a link back to the thread’s category using thread.category_id.
Shows the thread title, prefix, author, creation time, reply count, lock status, and last update time.
event_handlers: onClickAuthor() -> navigates to user_profile.html?profileId={thread.author_profile_id} when author_profile_id is available.

components.functionality: Provides actions for the thread: subscribe/unsubscribe, reply shortcut (scrolls to reply form), and sort direction selection for posts.
event_handlers: onClickSubscribeToggle() -> determines subscribe = !thread.is_subscribed, calls toggleThreadSubscription({threadId: thread.id, subscribe}), updates displayed subscription state using response.is_subscribed.

onChangeSortDirection(newSortDirection) -> updates sort_direction in the URL (and resets page if needed), triggers thread-data-loader via URL change to call getForumThreadView with the new sort_direction.
onClickReply() -> scrolls or focuses the reply-form component; does not call an interface directly.

components.functionality: Renders the list of posts for the current page of the thread in the order provided by getForumThreadView, and exposes interactions on each post.
event_handlers: onClickPostAuthor(post) -> navigates to user_profile.html?profileId={post.author_profile_id} when author_profile_id is available.
onClickPostTime(post) -> optionally updates the URL with a fragment or query to indicate a specific post; does not call any interface.
onClickQuote(post) -> optionally pre-fills the reply-form body with a reference to post.body; does not call any interface.

components.functionality: Displays pagination controls (current page, total pages, next/previous) based on the pagination object returned by getForumThreadView, and updates URL parameters when users change pages or page size.
event_handlers: onClickPage(targetPage) -> updates page in the URL to targetPage and triggers thread-data-loader to call getForumThreadView.
onChangePageSize(newPageSize) -> updates page_size in the URL, resets page if necessary, and triggers thread-data-loader to call getForumThreadView with the new page_size.

components.functionality: Allows the user to write and submit a reply to the current thread. Disables input and submission if thread.is_locked is true.
event_handlers: onSubmit(body) -> if thread.is_locked is false, calls replyToForumThread({threadId: thread.id, body}), then on success either appends the returned post to the post-list (if it belongs to the current page) or updates the URL to show the page containing the new post and triggers thread-data-loader to refresh via getForumThreadView.
onChangeBody() -> updates the local form state; does not call any interface.

components.functionality: Displays the current subscription state of the thread (subscribed or not) and any status messages returned by toggleThreadSubscription.
event_handlers: onSubscriptionUpdated(is_subscribed) -> updates the displayed status when thread-actions-bar receives a successful toggleThreadSubscription response.

components.functionality: Provides a global search input on the page header. Uses searchSite to retrieve matching programs and forum threads for the entered query.
event_handlers: onSubmitSearch(query, type_filter, page, page_size) -> calls searchSite({query, type_filter, page, page_size}), then optionally displays a brief inline summary of results or navigates to search_results.html with query and other search parameters in the URL.
onChangeQuery() -> updates the local search input state; does not call an interface until submission.

layout.grid_system: Implicit single-column fluid layout, likely based on simple block elements or a basic HTML table for some parts rather than a formal grid system.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a simple, single-column, top-to-bottom document flow. At the very top, the thread header appears on the left with the global search form aligned to the right on the same horizontal line, mimicking the login strip behavior from the mockup. Directly beneath this row, the breadcrumb navigation spans the full width. Below the breadcrumb, a large, bordered content box fills most of the page height. Inside this box, the thread actions bar and subscription status sit at the top as a thin horizontal strip, followed by pagination controls, then the vertically stacked list of posts, another pagination strip, and finally the reply form at the bottom.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a simple, single-column, top-to-bottom document flow. At the very top, the thread header appears on the left with the global search form aligned to the right on the same horizontal line, mimicking the login strip behavior from the mockup. Directly beneath this row, the breadcrumb navigation spans the full width. Below the breadcrumb, a large, bordered content box fills most of the page height. Inside this box, the thread actions bar and subscription status sit at the top as a thin horizontal strip, followed by pagination controls, then the vertically stacked list of posts, another pagination strip, and finally the reply form at the bottom.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: A modest outer margin (roughly 16–24px) separates the main content column from the browser edges. The header/search row and breadcrumb occupy minimal vertical space at the top. The main bordered thread box occupies the majority of vertical space, stretching nearly the full width of the content column. Within this box, vertical spacing between internal elements (actions bar, pagination, posts list, reply form) is compact (8–12px). Each post uses nearly the full width of the container but with only about 8–12px padding on the left and right.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout is intentionally dense. Line-height is tight, with minimal vertical spacing between text lines and between consecutive posts. Controls (inputs, buttons, select elements) use minimal internal padding (2–4px). Section gaps between stacked elements (header/search row, breadcrumb, main box) are small (8–12px), emphasizing content over whitespace. The posts list occupies most of the viewport to show as many posts as possible without scrolling, with no decorative blocks or extra vertical breathing room.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow starts at the bold thread title on the top-left, then tracks to the right along the global search form on the same line. The eye then moves straight down to the breadcrumb, then into the large bordered thread box. Inside this box, the eye goes from the left-aligned actions bar to the right-aligned subscription text, then down to the pagination links, and then flows line by line through the stacked posts from top to bottom. Within each post, scanning is left to right (username and metadata, then message), but the primary movement is a strong vertical descent through the post list, ending at the reply form at the bottom of the box.

layout.overall_description: The page looks like a classic early‑2000s forum thread: a plain white background, unbranded, with mostly default browser styling and simple borders. At the very top, the thread title appears in bold text on the left, while a compact search form sits on the right on the same line, both aligned to the main content’s left margin. Just below, a single breadcrumb line runs across the width, using small, plain text links. Beneath this, nearly the entire remaining viewport is dominated by a single, light‑bordered rectangular box that stretches almost the full width of the page. The top edge of this box contains a thin horizontal strip with sorting and thread actions on the left and a small subscription status text on the right. Immediately under that sit tightly packed pagination links. The majority of the box is filled by a dense vertical stack of posts, each separated by a subtle rule or small gap, with no decorative elements—only text, basic links, and simple form controls. At the very bottom of this big box, another pagination strip appears followed by a compact reply form. The result is a utilitarian, content‑first thread layout where the eye travels straight down the page through dense discussions with minimal distraction.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader is non-visual and functions as an invisible wrapper around the entire thread area. Conceptually, it sits just inside the main page content area, above the breadcrumb, thread header, actions bar, posts, pagination, and reply form. It occupies no visible width or height and does not affect spacing; instead, it ensures that when data is loaded, all visible components (breadcrumb, header, actions, post list, pagination, reply form, subscription status) are populated and rendered in their positions within the single-column vertical flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendered wrapper; participates only in logical structure, not in visual layout. It does not alter spacing or flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The breadcrumb navigation is a single horizontal line of small, plain text links positioned near the top of the page, directly below the thread header/global search row. It starts flush with the left content margin and spans nearly the full width of the page, with the text left-aligned.
